{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HVrSYAEtwQpTVm8fkCg4CM","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# CymClaw\n     2→\n     3→[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache_2.0-blue)](LICENSE)\n     4→[![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-alpha-orange)](docs/quickstart.md)\n     5→\n     6→CymClaw runs [OpenClaw](https://openclaw.ai) always-on assistants safely inside Docker, with inference routed through [Google AI](https://ai.google.dev) (Gemini 3 Flash Preview). It is a fork of NVIDIA NemoClaw that replaces OpenShell with pure Docker.\n     7→\n     8→> **Alpha software** — interfaces and behavior may change without notice.\n     9→\n    10→---\n    11→\n    12→## Quick Start\n    13→\n    14→```bash\n    15→curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cympack/cymclaw/main/install.sh | bash\n    16→```\n    17→\n    18→Or from source:\n    19→\n    20→```bash\n    21→git clone https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw.git\n    22→cd cymclaw\n    23→bash install.sh\n    24→```\n    25→\n    26→After install:\n    27→\n    28→```bash\n    29→cymclaw start      # start sandbox + gateway\n    30→cymclaw connect    # shell into sandbox\n    31→```\n    32→\n    33→Inside the sandbox:\n    34→\n    35→```bash\n    36→openclaw tui       # interactive chat with the agent\n    37→```\n    38→\n    39→---\n    40→\n    41→## Requirements\n    42→\n    43→| Resource | Minimum | Recommended |\n    44→|----------|---------|-------------|\n    45→| CPU      | 2 vCPU  | 4+ vCPU     |\n    46→| RAM      | 4 GB    | 8 GB        |\n    47→| Disk     | 5 GB    | 10 GB       |\n    48→\n    49→| Dependency | Version |\n    50→|------------|---------|\n    51→| Docker     | 24+     |\n    52→| Node.js    | 22+     |\n    53→| pnpm       | 9+      |\n    54→\n    55→Supported platforms: Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel).\n    56→\n    57→---\n    58→\n    59→## How It Works\n    60→\n    61→```\n    62→cymclaw start\n    63→    │\n    64→    ├── docker run cymclaw-sandbox   (--network cymclaw-isolated --read-only --seccomp)\n    65→    │       OpenClaw agent inside\n    66→    │       OPENAI_BASE_URL → gateway\n    67→    │\n    68→    └── node gateway/server.js       (host, port 8899)\n    69→            ↓ whitelist check\n    70→            ↓ key injection\n    71→        https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/\n    72→```\n    73→\n    74→All inference calls from the sandbox go through the host-side gateway, which:\n    75→1. Checks the request host against the whitelist\n    76→2. Injects the Gemini API key\n    77→3. Forwards to Gemini's OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n    78→\n    79→The sandbox container never has direct internet access (Docker `--internal` network).\n    80→\n    81→---\n    82→\n    83→## Security Layers\n    84→\n    85→| Layer      | Mechanism                               | Notes                          |\n    86→|------------|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------|\n    87→| Network    | Docker `--internal` bridge + gateway proxy | iptables rules on Linux too  |\n    88→| Filesystem | `--read-only` + `--tmpfs /tmp`          | Write only to `/sandbox`       |\n    89→| Process    | `--security-opt seccomp=cymclaw-seccomp.json` | Blocks dangerous syscalls |\n    90→| Privileges | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` + `USER sandbox` | Non-root user |\n    91→| Inference  | Gateway proxy intercepts all AI calls   | Whitelist + key injection      |\n    92→\n    93→---\n    94→\n    95→## CLI Reference\n    96→\n    97→```\n    98→cymclaw install          First-time setup wizard\n    99→cymclaw start            Start sandbox + gateway\n   100→cymclaw stop             Stop everything\n   101→cymclaw connect          Open shell inside sandbox\n   102→cymclaw status           Show running status\n   103→cymclaw logs [-f]        Stream sandbox logs\n   104→cymclaw config           Edit config interactively\n   105→cymclaw ui               Open web config UI (localhost:3847)\n   106→cymclaw policy add <host>   Add host to network whitelist\n   107→cymclaw policy list         List allowed endpoints\n   108→cymclaw policy remove <host> Remove from whitelist\n   109→cymclaw uninstall        Remove CymClaw\n   110→```\n   111→\n   112→---\n   113→\n   114→## Configuration\n   115→\n   116→Config is stored at `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600).\n   117→\n   118→| Key | Default | Description |\n   119→|-----|---------|-------------|\n   120→| `geminiApiKey` | — | Google AI API key |\n   121→| `model` | `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` | Gemini model |\n   122→| `gatewayPort` | `8899` | Gateway listen port |\n   123→| `uiPort` | `3847` | Web UI port |\n   124→| `networkWhitelist` | see defaults | Allowed outbound hosts |\n   125→| `logAudit` | `true` | Log requests to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log` |\n   126→\n   127→Get a free Gemini API key at [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey).\n   128→\n   129→---\n   130→\n   131→## Web UI\n   132→\n   133→```bash\n   134→cymclaw ui   # opens http://localhost:3847\n   135→```\n   136→\n   137→Features:\n   138→- View sandbox + gateway status\n   139→- Edit Gemini API key and model\n   140→- Manage network whitelist (add/remove hosts)\n   141→- View audit log of recent inference calls\n   142→- Start/stop sandbox\n   143→\n   144→---\n   145→\n   146→## Network Whitelist Presets\n   147→\n   148→| Preset | Included hosts |\n   149→|--------|---------------|\n   150→| `default` | Gemini, Anthropic, OpenClaw, GitHub, npm |\n   151→| `cympack` | default + Jina AI, Pacdora |\n   152→| `slack` | default + Slack API |\n   153→| `telegram` | default + Telegram Bot API |\n   154→\n   155→---\n   156→\n   157→## Learn More\n   158→\n   159→- [Quickstart](docs/quickstart.md)\n   160→- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)\n   161→- [Security](docs/security.md)\n   162→- [Configuration](docs/configuration.md)\n   163→\n   164→## License\n   165→\n   166→Apache License 2.0 — see [LICENSE](LICENSE).\n   167→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WK7f4AqCtkdbqucfLL1ofX","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#!/usr/bin/env bash\n     2→# CymClaw installer — Ubuntu 22.04+ and macOS (Apple Silicon)\n     3→# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     4→#\n     5→# Usage:\n     6→#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cympack/cymclaw/main/install.sh | bash\n     7→#   or locally: bash install.sh\n     8→\n     9→set -euo pipefail\n    10→\n    11→info()  { printf '\\033[1;34m[INFO]\\033[0m  %s\\n' \"$*\"; }\n    12→warn()  { printf '\\033[1;33m[WARN]\\033[0m  %s\\n' \"$*\"; }\n    13→error() { printf '\\033[1;31m[ERROR]\\033[0m %s\\n' \"$*\"; exit 1; }\n    14→\n    15→command_exists() { command -v \"$1\" &>/dev/null; }\n    16→\n    17→MIN_NODE_MAJOR=22\n    18→CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR=\"${HOME}/.local/bin\"\n    19→ORIGINAL_PATH=\"${PATH:-}\"\n    20→\n    21→version_major() { printf '%s\\n' \"${1#v}\" | cut -d. -f1; }\n    22→\n    23→version_gte() {\n    24→  local IFS=.\n    25→  # shellcheck disable=SC2206\n    26→  local -a a=($1) b=($2)\n    27→  for i in 0 1 2; do\n    28→    local ai=${a[$i]:-0} bi=${b[$i]:-0}\n    29→    (( ai > bi )) && return 0\n    30→    (( ai < bi )) && return 1\n    31→  done\n    32→  return 0\n    33→}\n    34→\n    35→ensure_nvm_loaded() {\n    36→  if [[ -z \"${NVM_DIR:-}\" ]]; then export NVM_DIR=\"$HOME/.nvm\"; fi\n    37→  if [[ -s \"$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh\" ]]; then \\. \"$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh\"; fi\n    38→}\n    39→\n    40→refresh_path() {\n    41→  ensure_nvm_loaded\n    42→  local npm_bin\n    43→  npm_bin=\"$(npm config get prefix 2>/dev/null)/bin\" || true\n    44→  if [[ -n \"$npm_bin\" && -d \"$npm_bin\" && \":$PATH:\" != *\":$npm_bin:\"* ]]; then\n    45→    export PATH=\"$npm_bin:$PATH\"\n    46→  fi\n    47→  if [[ -d \"$CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR\" && \":$PATH:\" != *\":$CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR:\"* ]]; then\n    48→    export PATH=\"$CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR:$PATH\"\n    49→  fi\n    50→}\n    51→\n    52→# ── 1. Docker ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    53→install_docker_linux() {\n    54→  if command_exists docker; then\n    55→    info \"Docker found: $(docker --version)\"\n    56→    return\n    57→  fi\n    58→  info \"Installing Docker...\"\n    59→  curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh\n    60→  sudo usermod -aG docker \"$USER\" || true\n    61→  info \"Docker installed. You may need to log out and back in for group membership.\"\n    62→  # Start Docker service\n    63→  sudo systemctl enable --now docker || true\n    64→}\n    65→\n    66→install_docker_macos() {\n    67→  if command_exists docker; then\n    68→    info \"Docker found: $(docker --version)\"\n    69→    return\n    70→  fi\n    71→  if command_exists brew; then\n    72→    info \"Installing Docker Desktop via Homebrew...\"\n    73→    brew install --cask docker\n    74→    info \"Docker Desktop installed. Please open Docker.app to complete setup, then re-run this script.\"\n    75→    exit 0\n    76→  else\n    77→    error \"Docker not found. Install Docker Desktop from https://docs.docker.com/desktop/mac/install/\"\n    78→  fi\n    79→}\n    80→\n    81→ensure_docker() {\n    82→  local os\n    83→  os=\"$(uname -s)\"\n    84→  if [[ \"$os\" == \"Darwin\" ]]; then\n    85→    install_docker_macos\n    86→  else\n    87→    install_docker_linux\n    88→  fi\n    89→  # Wait for Docker daemon\n    90→  local retries=0\n    91→  while ! docker info &>/dev/null; do\n    92→    retries=$(( retries + 1 ))\n    93→    if (( retries > 15 )); then\n    94→      error \"Docker daemon is not running. Start Docker and retry.\"\n    95→    fi\n    96→    info \"Waiting for Docker daemon...\"\n    97→    sleep 2\n    98→  done\n    99→  info \"Docker daemon is running\"\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→# ── 2. Node.js ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   103→install_nodejs() {\n   104→  ensure_nvm_loaded\n   105→  if command_exists node; then\n   106→    local major\n   107→    major=\"$(version_major \"$(node --version)\")\"\n   108→    if (( major >= MIN_NODE_MAJOR )); then\n   109→      info \"Node.js OK: $(node --version)\"\n   110→      return\n   111→    fi\n   112→    warn \"Node.js $(node --version) is below required v${MIN_NODE_MAJOR}\"\n   113→  fi\n   114→  info \"Installing Node.js v${MIN_NODE_MAJOR} via nvm...\"\n   115→  if [[ ! -s \"${NVM_DIR:-$HOME/.nvm}/nvm.sh\" ]]; then\n   116→    local NVM_VERSION=\"v0.40.4\"\n   117→    local NVM_SHA256=\"4b7412c49960c7d31e8df72da90c1fb5b8cccb419ac99537b737028d497aba4f\"\n   118→    local nvm_tmp\n   119→    nvm_tmp=\"$(mktemp)\"\n   120→    curl -fsSL \"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/${NVM_VERSION}/install.sh\" -o \"$nvm_tmp\"\n   121→    local actual_hash\n   122→    if command_exists sha256sum; then\n   123→      actual_hash=\"$(sha256sum \"$nvm_tmp\" | awk '{print $1}')\"\n   124→    elif command_exists shasum; then\n   125→      actual_hash=\"$(shasum -a 256 \"$nvm_tmp\" | awk '{print $1}')\"\n   126→    else\n   127→      warn \"No SHA-256 tool found — skipping integrity check\"\n   128→      actual_hash=\"$NVM_SHA256\"\n   129→    fi\n   130→    if [[ \"$actual_hash\" != \"$NVM_SHA256\" ]]; then\n   131→      rm -f \"$nvm_tmp\"\n   132→      error \"nvm installer integrity check failed\\n  Expected: $NVM_SHA256\\n  Actual:   $actual_hash\"\n   133→    fi\n   134→    bash \"$nvm_tmp\"\n   135→    rm -f \"$nvm_tmp\"\n   136→    ensure_nvm_loaded\n   137→  fi\n   138→  nvm install \"${MIN_NODE_MAJOR}\"\n   139→  info \"Node.js installed: $(node --version)\"\n   140→}\n   141→\n   142→# ── 3. pnpm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   143→ensure_pnpm() {\n   144→  if command_exists pnpm; then\n   145→    info \"pnpm found: $(pnpm --version)\"; return\n   146→  fi\n   147→  info \"Installing pnpm...\"\n   148→  npm install -g pnpm\n   149→  refresh_path\n   150→  info \"pnpm installed: $(pnpm --version)\"\n   151→}\n   152→\n   153→# ── 4. CymClaw ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   154→install_cymclaw() {\n   155→  if [[ -f \"./package.json\" ]] && grep -q '\"name\": \"cymclaw\"' ./package.json 2>/dev/null; then\n   156→    info \"Installing CymClaw from source...\"\n   157→    pnpm install\n   158→    pnpm link --global\n   159→  else\n   160→    info \"Installing CymClaw from GitHub...\"\n   161→    pnpm install -g git+https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw.git\n   162→  fi\n   163→  refresh_path\n   164→  # Create shim if npm global bin not on PATH\n   165→  local npm_bin\n   166→  npm_bin=\"$(npm config get prefix 2>/dev/null)/bin\" || true\n   167→  if [[ -n \"$npm_bin\" && -x \"$npm_bin/cymclaw\" ]]; then\n   168→    if [[ \":$ORIGINAL_PATH:\" != *\":$npm_bin:\"* ]]; then\n   169→      mkdir -p \"$CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR\"\n   170→      ln -sfn \"$npm_bin/cymclaw\" \"${CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR}/cymclaw\"\n   171→      refresh_path\n   172→    fi\n   173→  fi\n   174→}\n   175→\n   176→verify_cymclaw() {\n   177→  if command_exists cymclaw; then\n   178→    info \"Verified: cymclaw at $(command -v cymclaw)\"\n   179→    return 0\n   180→  fi\n   181→  warn \"cymclaw not found on PATH after install.\"\n   182→  warn \"Add to your shell profile: export PATH=\\\"\\$HOME/.local/bin:\\$PATH\\\"\"\n   183→  warn \"Then run: source ~/.bashrc  (or ~/.zshrc)\"\n   184→  return 1\n   185→}\n   186→\n   187→# ── 5. 46→function readBody(req) {\n    47→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    48→    const chunks = [];\n    49→    req.on('data', (c) => chunks.push(c));\n    50→    req.on('end', () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n    51→    req.on('error', reject);\n    52→  });\n    53→}\n    54→\n    55→function isWhitelisted(targetHost) {\n    56→  if (WHITELIST.has(targetHost)) return (${body.length}B)`);\n    98→\n    99→  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n   100→    const upstreamReq = https.request({\n   101→      hostname: GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host,\n   102→      port: (!res.headersSent) {\n   116→        res.writeHead(502, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n   117→        res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: { message: 'Gateway upstream error', type: ── Proxy to arbitrary whitelisted host ────────────────────────────\n   128→async function proxyToHost(req, res, body, targetHost) {\n   129→  const parsedUrl = url.parse(req.url);\n   130→  const targetPath = parsedUrl.path || '/';\n   131→\n   132→  const upstreamHeaders = { ...req.headers, host: targetHost };\n   133→  ['connection', 'keep-alive', 'transfer-encoding'].forEach((h) => delete upstreamHeaders[h]);\n   134→  if (body.length > 0) upstreamHeaders['content-length'] = body.length;\n   135→\n   136→  auditLog(`ALLOW ${req.method} ${targetHost}${targetPath} (${body.length}B)`);\n   137→\n   138→  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n   139→    const upstreamReq = https.request({\n   140→      hostname: targetHost,\n   141→      port: 443,\n   142→      path: targetPath,\n   143→ upstreamReq.end();\n   159→  });\n   160→}\n   161→\n   162→// ── Main handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   163→async function handleRequest(req, res) {\n   164→  if 199→  handleRequest(req, res).catch((err) => {\n   200→    console.error(`[gateway] unhandled error: ${err.message}`);\n   201→    if (!res.headersSent) {\n   202→      res.writeHead(500); {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJ5i3BTErc3gWWAf38zZz1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→// CymClaw — Docker helpers\n     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     3→\n     4→'use strict';\n     5→\n     6→const { execSync } = require('child_process');\n     7→\n     8→function dockerRun(args, opts = {}) {\n     9→  const cmd = `docker run ${args}`;\n    10→  return _exec(cmd, opts);\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→function dockerPs(name) {\n    14→  try {\n    15→    const out = execSync(`docker ps --filter name=^/${name}$ --format '{{.Names}}'`, {\n    16→      encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],\n    17→    }).trim();\n    18→    return out === name;\n    19→  } catch {\n    20→    return false;\n    21→  }\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→function dockerStop(name, opts = {}) {\n    25→  return _exec(`docker stop ${name}`, opts);\n    26→}\n    27→\n    28→function dockerRm(name, opts = {}) {\n    29→  return _exec(`docker rm -f ${name}`, opts);\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→function dockerExec(name, shellCmd, opts = {}) {\n    33→  return _exec(`docker exec ${name} ${shellCmd}`, opts);\n    34→}\n    35→\n    36→function dockerLogs(name, follow = false) {\n    37→  const flag = follow ? ' -f' : '';\n    38→  return _exec(`docker logs${flag} ${name}`, { stdio: 'inherit' });\n    39→}\n    40→\n    41→function dockerNetworkExists(name) {\n    42→  try {\n    43→    execSync(`docker network inspect ${name}`, { stdio: 'pipe' });\n    44→    return true;\n    45→  } catch {\n    46→    return false;\n    47→  }\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→function dockerNetworkCreate(name, opts = '') {\n    51→  return _exec(`docker network create ${opts} ${name}`, { ignoreError: true });\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→function dockerInspect(name, format) {\n    55→  try {\n    56→    return execSync(`docker inspect ${name} --format '${format}'`, {\n    57→      encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],\n    58→    }).trim();\n    59→  } catch {\n    60→    return '';\n    61→  }\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→function dockerBuild(contextPath, tag, opts = '') {\n    65→  return _exec(`docker build ${opts} -t ${tag} ${contextPath}`, { stdio: 'inherit' });\n    66→}\n    67→\n    68→function dockerVolumeCreate(name) {\n    69→  return _exec(`docker volume create ${name}`, { ignoreError: true });\n    70→}\n    71→\n    72→function _exec(cmd, opts = {}) {\n    73→  const { spawnSync } = require('child_process');\n    74→  const result = spawnSync('bash', ['-c', cmd], {\n    75→    stdio: opts.stdio ?? Run: cymclaw install');\n    27→    process.exit(1);\n    28→  }\n    29→\n    30→  // Ensure network exists (may have been removed)\n    31→  if (!dockerNetworkExists(NETWORK_NAME)) {\n    32→    dockerNetworkCreate(NETWORK_NAME, '--driver bridge --internal');\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  // Ensure workspace volume\n    36→  dockerVolumeCreate('cymclaw-workspace');\n    37→\n    38→  // Stop existing containers cleanly\n    39→  if (dockerPs(SANDBOX_NAME)) {\n    40→    console.log(`  Stopping existing sandbox...`);\n    41→    dockerStop(SANDBOX_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n    42→    dockerRm(SANDBOX_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n    43→  }\n    44→  if (dockerPs(GATEWAY_NAME)) {\n    45→    dockerStop(GATEWAY_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n    46→    dockerRm(GATEWAY_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n    47→  }\n    48→\n    49→  // ── Start gateway ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n    50→  console.log(`  Starting inference gateway on port ${GATEWAY_PORT}...`);\n    51→  const gatewayDir = path.join(ROOT, 'gateway');\n    52→\n    53→  // The gateway runs as a plain Node.js process on the host (not in Docker)\n    54→  // so it can reach the internet and forward to Gemini while the sandbox\n    55→  // is --network=none / internal-only.\n    56→  const gatewayEnv = {\n    57→    ...process.env,\n    58→    GEMINI_API_KEY: cfg.geminiApiKey,\n    59→    GEMINI_MODEL: cfg.model,\n    60→    GATEWAY_PORT: String(cfg.gatewayPort || GATEWAY_PORT),\n    61→    WHITELIST: (cfg.networkWhitelist || []).join(','),\n    62→    AUDIT_LOG: cfg.logAudit ? '1' : '0',\n    63→    AUDIT_LOG_PATH: path.join(CONFIG_DIR, 'audit.log'),\n    64→  };\n    65→\n    66→  const gatewayProc = spawn('node', [path.join(gatewayDir, 'server.js')], {\n    67→    env: gatewayEnv,\n    68→    detached: true,\n    69→    stdio: ['ignore', 'ignore', 'ignore'],\n    70→  });\n    71→  gatewayProc.unref();\n    72→\n    73→  // Save gateway PID\n    74→  fs.mkdirSync(CONFIG_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    75→  fs.writeFileSync(path.join(CONFIG_DIR, 'gateway.pid'), String(gatewayProc.pid), { mode: 0o600 });\n    76→\n    77→  // Brief wait for gateway to bind\n    78→  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 800));\n    79→  console.log(`  \\x1b[32m✓\\x1b[0m Gateway started (pid ${gatewayProc.pid})`);\n    80→\n    81→  // ── Start sandbox   85→  const hasSeccomp  = fs.existsSync(seccompPath);\n    86→\n    87→  // Get host gateway IP (the gateway is on the host, reachable from bridge)\n    88→  let hostGatewayIp = '172.17.0.1'; // ['pipe','pipe','pipe'],\n    92→    }).trim();\n    93→    if (info) hostGatewayIp = info;\n    94→  } catch {}\n    95→\n    96→  const seccompOpt = hasSeccomp\n    97→    ? `--security-opt {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BmJXiQa9op4XUE44D337Qs","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→// CymClaw — web UI launcher\n     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     3→\n     4→'use strict';\n     5→\n     6→const http   = require('http');\n     7→const fs     = require('fs');\n     8→const path   = require('path');\n     9→const { execSync } = require('child_process');\n    10→const { loadConfig, saveConfig } = require('./config');\n    11→const { ROOT } = require('./runner');\n    12→const { UI_PORT } = require('./constants');\n    13→\n    14→async function launchUi() {\n    15→  const cfg = loadConfig();\n    16→  const port = cfg.uiPort || UI_PORT;\n    17→  const uiDir = path.join(ROOT, 'ui');\n    18→\n    19→  const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {\n    20→    // API endpoints\n    21→    if (req.method === 'GET' && req.url === '/api/config') {\n    22→      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n    23→      res.end(JSON.stringify(loadConfig()));\n    24→      return;\n    25→    }\n    26→\n    27→    if (req.method === 'POST' && req.url === '/api/config') {\n    28→      let body = '';\n    29→      req.on('data', (c) => (body += c));\n    30→      req.on('end', () => {\n    31→        try {\n    32→          const incoming = JSON.parse(body);\n    33→          const current = loadConfig();\n    34→          saveConfig({ ...current, ...incoming });\n    35→          res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n    36→          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }));\n    37→        } catch {\n    38→          res.writeHead(400); res.end('Bad Request');\n    39→        }\n    40→      });\n    41→      return;\n    42→    }\n    43→\n    44→    if (req.method === 'GET' && req.url === '/api/status') {\n    45→      const { dockerPs } = require('./docker');\n    46→      const { SANDBOX_NAME, GATEWAY_PORT } = require('./constants');\n    47→      const sandboxRunning = dockerPs(SANDBOX_NAME);\n    48→      const auditPath = path.join(require('./config').CONFIG_DIR, 'audit.log');\n    49→      let recentLogs = [];\n    50→      if (fs.existsSync(auditPath)) {\n    51→        recentLogs = fs.readFileSync(auditPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\\n').filter(Boolean).slice(-20);\n    52→      }\n    53→      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n    54→      res.end(JSON.stringify({ sandboxRunning, recentLogs }));\n    55→      return;\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Static files\n    59→    let filePath = req.url === '/' ? '/index.html' : req.url;\n    60→    filePath = path.join(uiDir, filePath);\n    61→    if (!filePath.startsWith(uiDir)) { res.writeHead(403); res.end(); return; }\n    62→    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) { res.writeHead(404); res.end('Not found'); return; }\n    63→\n    64→    const ext = path.extname(filePath);\n    65→    const types = { '.html': 'text/html', '.js': 'application/javascript', '.css': 'text/css' };\n    66→    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': types[ext] || 'text/plain' });\n    67→    fs.createReadStream(filePath).pipe(res);\n    68→  });\n    69→\n    70→  server.listen(port, '127.0.0.1', () => {\n    71→    const url = `http://localhost:${port}`;\n    72→    console.log(`  \\x1b[32m✓\\x1b[0m CymClaw UI running at \\x1b[34m${url}\\x1b[0m`);\n    73→    console.log('  Press Ctrl+C to stop the UI server.');\n    74→    // Open browser\n    75→    try {\n    76→      const opener = process.platform === 'darwin' ? 'open' : 'xdg-open';\n    77→      execSync(`${opener} ${url}`, { stdio: 'ignore' });\n    78→    } catch {}\n    79→  });\n    80→\n    81→  // Keep alive\n    82→  await new Promise(() => {});\n    83→}\n    84→\n    85→module.exports = { launchUi };\n    86→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01J4cYig8qsjdXhwmpCsyZuz","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# CymClaw Architecture\n     2→\n     3→## Overview\n     4→\n     5→CymClaw is a thin CLI that orchestrates two components:\n     6→\n     7→1. **Sandbox** — a Docker container running OpenClaw with filesystem and network isolation\n     8→2. **Gateway** — a Node.js HTTP proxy on the host that routes inference calls to Gemini\n     9→\n    10→```\n    11→Host\n    12→├── cymclaw CLI (Node.js)\n    13→│   ├── bin/cymclaw.js          — command dispatch\n    14→│   └── bin/lib/\n    15→│       ├── install.js          — setup wizard\n    16→│       ├── start.js            — start sandbox + gateway\n    17→│       ├── docker.js           — Docker helpers\n    18→│       ├── config.js           — config (~/. cymclaw/config.json)\n    19→│       └── ...\n    20→│\n    21→├── gateway/server.js            — inference proxy (port 8899)\n    22→│   ├── Whitelist enforcement\n    23→│   ├── API key injection\n    24→│   └── Audit logging\n    25→│\n    26→└── Docker\n    27→    ├── Network: cymclaw-isolated  (--internal bridge, no direct internet)\n    28→    └── Container: cymclaw-sandbox\n    29→        ├── OpenClaw pre-installed\n    30→        ├── User: sandbox (non-root)\n    31→        ├── --read-only filesystem\n    32→        ├── --tmpfs /tmp\n    33→        └── --security-opt seccomp=cymclaw-seccomp.json\n    34→```\n    35→\n    36→## Inference Routing\n    37→\n    38→```\n    39→OpenClaw agent (in sandbox)\n    40→    │  OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://cymclaw-gateway:8899/v1\n    41→    ▼\n    42→cymclaw-gateway (host, port 8899)\n    43→    │  check whitelist\n    44→    │  inject GEMINI_API_KEY\n    45→    ▼\n    46→https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/\n    47→    (Gemini OpenAI-compatible endpoint)\n    48→```\n    49→\n    50→The sandbox sets `OPENAI_BASE_URL` to the gateway. OpenClaw uses OpenAI SDK format, which the Gemini endpoint accepts.\n    51→\n    52→## Security Model\n    53→\n    54→### Network Isolation\n    55→- Docker network `cymclaw-isolated` is `--internal` (no outbound routing by Docker)\n    56→- On Linux, iptables rules in `CYMCLAW` chain allow only whitelisted IPs\n    57→- On macOS, the gateway proxy is the sole network egress path\n    58→- The gateway only forwards to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` (and whitelist additions)\n    59→\n    60→### Filesystem Isolation\n    61→- Container root filesystem is `--read-only`\n    62→- `/tmp` is a tmpfs (max 512 MB, exec allowed for builds)\n    63→- `/sandbox/workspace` is a persistent Docker volume\n    64→- All other paths read-only inside the image\n    65→\n    66→### Process Isolation\n    67→- Non-root user `sandbox` inside container\n    68→- `--security-opt no-new-privileges` prevents privilege escalation\n    69→- `--security-opt seccomp=cymclaw-seccomp.json` blocks dangerous syscalls (mount, reboot, kexec, BPF, etc.)\n    70→\n    71→## Config Lifecycle\n    72→\n    73→```\n    74→~/.cymclaw/\n    75→├── config.json        (600) — API key, model, whitelist\n    76→├── gateway.pid        (600) — gateway process ID\n    77→├── cymclaw-seccomp.json     — seccomp profile\n    78→└── audit.log               — gateway request log\n    79→```\n    80→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UcnQqg885FR9iFqvvADyYn","session_id":"c21e4ac7-d99c-4212-a75b-c69f1628ad13","uuid":"daf2df1a-ae39-472c-bba4-cd2d3fbd593e"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017zDhm8SFNNY9Cvea9VXT1M","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Security\n     2→\n     3→## Protection Layers\n     4→\n     5→| Layer | Mechanism | Strength |\n     6→|-------|-----------|----------|\n     7→| Network egress | Docker `--internal` + gateway whitelist | Strong on Linux (iptables); gateway-only on macOS |\n     8→| Filesystem | `--read-only` + tmpfs | Prevents persistent writes outside `/sandbox` |\n     9→| Process | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` | No privilege escalation |\n    10→| Syscalls | seccomp profile | Blocks 30+ dangerous syscalls |\n    11→| User | Non-root `sandbox` user | Limits damage if container breakout |\n    12→| Inference | Gateway key injection + logging | Agent never holds the real key |\n    13→\n    14→## Network Whitelist\n    15→\n    16→Default allowed hosts:\n    17→- `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` — Gemini API\n    18→- `api.anthropic.com` — OpenClaw\n    19→- `openclaw.ai`, `clawhub.com` — OpenClaw services\n    20→- `github.com`, `api.github.com` — git\n    21→- `registry.npmjs.org` — npm\n    22→\n    23→Add hosts with `cymclaw policy add <host>`.\n    24→\n    25→### macOS vs Linux\n    26→\n    27→On **Linux**, two layers enforce the whitelist:\n    28→1. The gateway proxy (software)\n    29→2. iptables rules in `CYMCLAW` chain (kernel)\n    30→\n    31→On **macOS**, Docker Desktop does not expose the kernel's network stack the same way. The Docker `--internal` network blocks direct outbound routing, and the gateway proxy is the sole allowed egress path. This is equivalent security for agent use.\n    32→\n    33→## Seccomp Profile\n    34→\n    35→`policies/cymclaw-seccomp.json` blocks syscalls including:\n    36→- `mount`, `umount` — filesystem mounting\n    37→- `kexec_load`, `kexec_file_load` — kernel replacement\n    38→- `init_module`, `finit_module` — kernel modules\n    39→- `reboot` — system reboot\n    40→- `bpf` — kernel eBPF programs\n    41→- `iopl`, `ioperm` — direct I/O port access\n    42→- `ptrace` — process tracing (partially restricted)\n    43→- `pivot_root` — container escape vector\n    44→\n    45→## API Key Security\n    46→\n    47→The Gemini API key is:\n    48→- Stored in `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600, readable only by owner)\n    49→- Injected into the sandbox at startup via `docker run -e`\n    50→- Never visible inside the container as a plain variable after startup (it is written into `openclaw.json` by `entrypoint.sh` and the env var is available in the container env — this is a known limitation of `docker run -e`)\n    51→\n    52→## Audit Log\n    53→\n    54→All inference requests through the gateway are logged to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log`. Each line includes timestamp, method, host, and path. The log is readable in `cymclaw status` and the web UI.\n    55→\n    56→## Reporting Issues\n    57→\n    58→File security issues at: https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw/security/advisories\n    59→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01UcnQqg885FR9iFqvvADyYn","session_id":"c21e4ac7-d99c-4212-a75b-c69f1628ad13","uuid":"e9a1c8be-6ad2-43d3-8c13-d2677bc74cc7"}

require('node:assert/strict');\n     9→\n    10→// Extract whitelist logic inline for testing (mirrors gateway/server.js)\n    11→function buildWhitelist(envVal) {\n    12→  return new Set([\n    13→    'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',\n    14→    ...(envVal ? envVal.split(',').map((h) => h.trim()).filter(Boolean) : []),\n    15→  ]);\n    16→}\n    17→\n    18→function isWhitelisted(whitelist, targetHost) {\n    19→  if (whitelist.has(targetHost)) return true;\n    20→  for (const h of whitelist) {\n    21→    if (h.startsWith('*.') && targetHost.endsWith(h.slice(1))) return true;\n    22→  }\n    23→  return false;\n    24→}\n    25→\n    26→describe('gateway whitelist', () => {\n    27→  it('always allows generativelanguage.googleapis.com', () => {\n    28→    const wl = buildWhitelist('');\n    34→    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'evil.example.com'));\n    35→    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'api.openai.com'));\n    36→    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'exfil.attacker.io'));\n    37→  });\n    38→\n    39→  it('allows hosts added via whitelist env', () => {\n    40→    const wl = buildWhitelist('api.github.com,registry.npmjs.org');\n    41→ assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'notgithub.com'));\n    50→  });\n    51→\n    52→  it('wildcard prefix matches subdomain suffix', () => {\n    53→    const wl = buildWhitelist('*.amazonaws.com');\n    54→    assert.ok(isWhitelisted(wl, 's3.amazonaws.com'));\n    55→    assert.ok(isWhitelisted(wl, 'ec2.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'));\n    56→    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'evil-amazonaws.com'));\n    57→  });\n    58→\n    59→  it('trims whitespace from whitelist entries', () => {\n    60→    const wl = buildWhitelist('  api.github.com , registry.npmjs.org  ');\n    61→ 90→| Privileges | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` + `USER sandbox` | Non-root user |\n    91→| Inference  | Gateway proxy intercepts all AI calls   | Whitelist + key injection      |\n    92→\n Checks the request host against the whitelist\n2. Injects the Gemini API key\n3. Forwards to Gemini's OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n\nThe sandbox container never has direct internet access (Docker `--internal` network).\n\n---\n\n## Security Layers\n\n| Layer      | Mechanism                               | Notes                          |\n|------------|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------|\n| Network    | Docker `--internal` bridge + gateway proxy | iptables rules on Linux too  |\n| Filesystem | `--read-only` + `--tmpfs /tmp`          | Write only to `/sandbox`       |\n| Process    | `--security-opt seccomp=cymclaw-seccomp.json` | Blocks dangerous syscalls |\n| Privileges | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` + `USER sandbox` | Non-root user |\n| Inference  | Gateway proxy intercepts all AI calls   | Whitelist + key injection      |\n\n---\n\n## CLI Reference\n\n```\ncymclaw install          First-time setup wizard\ncymclaw start            Start sandbox + gateway\ncymclaw stop             Stop everything\ncymclaw connect whitelist\ncymclaw uninstall        Remove CymClaw\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nConfig is stored at `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600).\n\n| Key | Default | Description Docker ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ninstall_docker_linux() {\n  if command_exists docker; then\n    info \"Docker found: $(docker --version)\"\n    return\n  fi\n  info \"Installing Docker...\"\n  curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh\n  sudo usermod -aG docker \"$USER\" || true\n  info \"Docker installed. You may need to log out and back in for group membership.\"\n  # Start Docker service\n  sudo systemctl enable --now docker || true\n}\n\ninstall_docker_macos() {\n  if command_exists docker; then\n    info \"Docker found: $(docker --version)\"\n    return\n  fi\n  if command_exists brew; then\n    info \"Installing Docker Desktop via Homebrew...\"\n    brew install --cask docker\n    info \"Docker Desktop installed. Please open Docker.app to complete setup, then re-run this script.\"\n    exit 0\n  else\n    error \"Docker not found. Install Docker Desktop from https://docs.docker.com/desktop/mac/install/\"\n  fi\n}\n\nensure_docker() {\n  local os\n  os=\"$(uname -s)\"\n  if [[ \"$os\" == \"Darwin\" ]]; then\n    install_docker_macos\n  else\n    install_docker_linux\n  fi\n  # Wait for Docker daemon\n  local retries=0\n  while ! docker info &>/dev/null; do\n    retries=$(( retries + 1 ))\n    if (( retries > 15 )); then\n      error \"Docker daemon is not running. Start Docker and retry.\"\n    fi\n    info \"Waiting for Docker daemon...\"\n    sleep 2\n  done\n  info \"Docker daemon is running\"\n}\n\n# ── 2. Node.js ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ninstall_nodejs() {\n  ensure_nvm_loaded\n  if command_exists node; then\n    local major\n    major=\"$(version_major \"$(node --version)\")\"\n    if (( major >= MIN_NODE_MAJOR )); then\n      info \"Node.js OK: $(node --version)\"\n      return\n    fi\n    warn \"Node.js $(node --version) is below required v${MIN_NODE_MAJOR}\"\n  fi\n  info \"Installing Node.js v${MIN_NODE_MAJOR} via nvm...\"\n  if [[ ! -s \"${NVM_DIR:-$HOME/.nvm}/nvm.sh\" ]]; then\n    local NVM_VERSION=\"v0.40.4\"\n    local NVM_SHA256=\"4b7412c49960c7d31e8df72da90c1fb5b8cccb419ac99537b737028d497aba4f\"\n    local nvm_tmp\n    nvm_tmp=\"$(mktemp)\"\n    curl -fsSL \"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/${NVM_VERSION}/install.sh\" -o \"$nvm_tmp\"\n    local actual_hash\n    if command_exists sha256sum; then\n      actual_hash=\"$(sha256sum \"$nvm_tmp\" | awk '{print $1}')\"\n    elif command_exists shasum; then\n      actual_hash=\"$(shasum -a 256 \"$nvm_tmp\" | awk '{print $1}')\"\n    else\n      warn \"No SHA-256 tool found — skipping integrity check\"\n      actual_hash=\"$NVM_SHA256\"\n    fi\n    if [[ \"$actual_hash\" != \"$NVM_SHA256\" ]]; then\n      rm -f \"$nvm_tmp\"\n      error \"nvm installer integrity check failed\\n  Expected: $NVM_SHA256\\n  Actual:   $actual_hash\"\n    fi\n    bash \"$nvm_tmp\"\n    rm -f \"$nvm_tmp\"\n    ensure_nvm_loaded\n  fi\n  nvm install \"${MIN_NODE_MAJOR}\"\n  info \"Node.js installed: $(node --version)\"\n}\n\n# ── 3. pnpm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nensure_pnpm() {\n  if command_exists pnpm; then\n    info \"pnpm found: $(pnpm --version)\"; return\n  fi\n  info \"Installing pnpm...\"\n  npm install -g pnpm\n  refresh_path\n  info \"pnpm installed: $(pnpm --version)\"\n}\n\n# ── 4. CymClaw ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ninstall_cymclaw() {\n  if [[ -f \"./package.json\" ]] && grep -q '\"name\": \"cymclaw\"' ./package.json 2>/dev/null; then\n    info \"Installing CymClaw from source...\"\n    pnpm install\n    pnpm link --global\n  else\n    info \"Installing CymClaw from GitHub...\"\n    pnpm install -g git+https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw.git\n  fi\n  refresh_path\n  # Create shim if npm global bin not on PATH\n  local npm_bin\n  npm_bin=\"$(npm config get prefix 2>/dev/null)/bin\" || true\n  if [[ -n \"$npm_bin\" && -x \"$npm_bin/cymclaw\" ]]; then\n    if [[ \":$ORIGINAL_PATH:\" != *\":$npm_bin:\"* ]]; then\n      mkdir -p \"$CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR\"\n      ln -sfn \"$npm_bin/cymclaw\" \"${CYMCLAW_SHIM_DIR}/cymclaw\"\n      refresh_path\n    fi\n  fi\n}\n\nverify_cymclaw() {\n  if command_exists cymclaw; then\n    info \"Verified: cymclaw at $(command -v cymclaw)\"\n    return 0\n  fi\n  warn \"cymclaw not found on PATH after install.\"\n  warn \"Add to your shell profile: export PATH=\\\"\\$HOME/.local/bin:\\$PATH\\\"\"\n  warn \"Then run: source ~/.bashrc  (or ~/.zshrc)\"\n  return 1\n}\n\n# ── 5. Onboard ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nrun_onboard() {\n  info \"Running cymclaw install...\"\n  if [[ \"${NON_INTERACTIVE:-}\" == \"1\" ]]; then\n    cymclaw install --non-interactive\n  elif [[ -t 0 ]]; then\n    cymclaw install\n  elif exec 3</dev/tty; then\n    info \"stdin is piped — attaching to /dev/tty...\"\n    cymclaw install <&3 || true\n    exec 3<&-\n  else\n    error \"Interactive setup requires a TTY. 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',\n    ...(envVal ? envVal.split(',').map((h) => h.trim()).filter(Boolean) : []),\n  ]);\n}\n\nfunction isWhitelisted(whitelist, targetHost) {\n  if res.end('Bad Request');\n        }\n      });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (req.method === 'GET' && req.url === '/api/status') {\n      const { dockerPs } = require('./docker');\n      const { SANDBOX_NAME, GATEWAY_PORT } = require('./constants');\n      const sandboxRunning = dockerPs(SANDBOX_NAME);\n      const auditPath = path.join(require('./config').CONFIG_DIR, 'audit.log');\n      let recentLogs = [];\n      if (fs.existsSync(auditPath)) {\n        recentLogs = fs.readFileSync(auditPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\\n').filter(Boolean).slice(-20);\n      }\n      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n      res.end(JSON.stringify({ sandboxRunning, recentLogs }));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Static files\n    let filePath = req.url === '/' ? '/index.html' : req.url;\n    filePath = path.join(uiDir, filePath);\n    if (!filePath.startsWith(uiDir)) { res.writeHead(403); res.end(); return; }\n    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) { res.writeHead(404); res.end('Not found'); return; }\n\n    const ext = path.extname(filePath);\n    const types = { '.html': 'text/html', '.js': 'application/javascript', '.css': 'text/css' };\n    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': types[ext] || 'text/plain' });\n    fs.createReadStream(filePath).pipe(res);\n  });\n\n  server.listen(port, '127.0.0.1', () => {\n    const url = `http://localhost:${port}`;\n    console.log(`  \\x1b[32m✓\\x1b[0m CymClaw UI running at \\x1b[34m${url}\\x1b[0m`);\n    console.log('  Press Ctrl+C to stop the UI server.');\n    // Open browser\n    try {\n      const opener = process.platform === 'darwin' ? 'open' : 'xdg-open';\n      execSync(`${opener} ${url}`, { stdio: 'ignore' });\n    } catch {}\n  });\n\n  // Keep alive\n  await new Promise(() => {});\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { launchUi };\n","numLines":86,"startLine":1,"totalLines":86}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017SRhQdCAZXy1d4rwb4Xp5t","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Security\n     2→\n     3→## Protection Layers\n     4→\n     5→| Layer | Mechanism | Strength |\n     6→|-------|-----------|----------|\n     7→| Network egress | Docker `--internal` + gateway whitelist | Strong on Linux (iptables); gateway-only on macOS |\n     8→| Filesystem | `--read-only` + tmpfs | Prevents persistent writes outside `/sandbox` |\n     9→| Process | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` | No privilege escalation |\n    10→| Syscalls | seccomp profile | Blocks 30+ dangerous syscalls |\n    11→| User | Non-root `sandbox` user | Limits damage if container breakout |\n    12→| Inference | Gateway key injection + logging | Agent never holds the real key |\n    13→\n    14→## Network Whitelist\n    15→\n    16→Default allowed hosts:\n    17→- `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` — Gemini API\n    18→- `api.anthropic.com` — OpenClaw\n    19→- `openclaw.ai`, `clawhub.com` — OpenClaw services\n    20→- `github.com`, `api.github.com` — git\n    21→- `registry.npmjs.org` — npm\n    22→\n    23→Add hosts with `cymclaw policy add <host>`.\n    24→\n    25→### macOS vs Linux\n    26→\n    27→On **Linux**, two layers enforce the whitelist:\n    28→1. The gateway proxy (software)\n    29→2. iptables rules in `CYMCLAW` chain (kernel)\n    30→\n    31→On **macOS**, Docker Desktop does not expose the kernel's network stack the same way. The Docker `--internal` network blocks direct outbound routing, and the gateway proxy is the sole allowed egress path. This is equivalent security for agent use.\n    32→\n    33→## Seccomp Profile\n    34→\n    35→`policies/cymclaw-seccomp.json` blocks syscalls including:\n    36→- `mount`, `umount` — filesystem mounting\n    37→- `kexec_load`, `kexec_file_load` — kernel replacement\n    38→- `init_module`, `finit_module` — kernel modules\n    39→- `reboot` — system reboot\n    40→- `bpf` — kernel eBPF programs\n    41→- `iopl`, `ioperm` — direct I/O port access\n    42→- `ptrace` — process tracing (partially restricted)\n    43→- `pivot_root` — container escape vector\n    44→\n    45→## API Key Security\n    46→\n    47→The Gemini API key is:\n    48→- Stored in `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600, readable only by owner)\n    49→- Injected into the sandbox at startup via `docker run -e`\n    50→- Never visible inside the container as a plain variable after startup (it is written into `openclaw.json` by `entrypoint.sh` and the env var is available in the container env — this is a known limitation of `docker run -e`)\n    51→\n    52→## Audit Log\n    53→\n    54→All inference requests through the gateway are logged to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log`. Each line includes timestamp, method, host, and path. The log is readable in `cymclaw status` and the web UI.\n    55→\n    56→## Reporting Issues\n    57→\n    58→File security issues at: https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw/security/advisories\n    59→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c21e4ac7-d99c-4212-a75b-c69f1628ad13","uuid":"647bdc4f-da7f-4501-ac3e-e4ae66419855","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/docs/security.md","content":"# Security\n\n## Protection Layers\n\n| Layer | Mechanism | Strength |\n|-------|-----------|----------|\n| Network egress | Docker `--internal` + gateway whitelist | Strong on Linux (iptables); gateway-only on macOS |\n| Filesystem | `--read-only` + tmpfs | Prevents persistent writes outside `/sandbox` |\n| Process | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` | No privilege escalation |\n| Syscalls | seccomp profile | Blocks 30+ dangerous syscalls |\n| User | Non-root `sandbox` user | Limits damage if container breakout |\n| Inference | Gateway key injection + logging | Agent never holds the real key |\n\n## Network Whitelist\n\nDefault allowed hosts:\n- `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` — Gemini API\n- `api.anthropic.com` — OpenClaw\n- `openclaw.ai`, `clawhub.com` — OpenClaw services\n- `github.com`, `api.github.com` — git\n- `registry.npmjs.org` — npm\n\nAdd hosts with `cymclaw policy add <host>`.\n\n### macOS vs Linux\n\nOn **Linux**, two layers enforce the whitelist:\n1. The gateway proxy (software)\n2. iptables rules in `CYMCLAW` chain (kernel)\n\nOn **macOS**, Docker Desktop does not expose the kernel's network stack the same way. The Docker `--internal` network blocks direct outbound routing, and the gateway proxy is the sole allowed egress path. This is equivalent security for agent use.\n\n## Seccomp Profile\n\n`policies/cymclaw-seccomp.json` blocks syscalls including:\n- `mount`, `umount` — filesystem mounting\n- `kexec_load`, `kexec_file_load` — kernel replacement\n- `init_module`, `finit_module` — kernel modules\n- `reboot` — system reboot\n- `bpf` — kernel eBPF programs\n- `iopl`, `ioperm` — direct I/O port access\n- `ptrace` — process tracing (partially restricted)\n- `pivot_root` — container escape vector\n\n## API Key Security\n\nThe Gemini API key is:\n- Stored in `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600, readable only by owner)\n- Injected into the sandbox at startup via `docker run -e`\n- Never visible inside the container as a plain variable after startup (it is written into `openclaw.json` by `entrypoint.sh` and the env var is available in the container env — this is a known limitation of `docker run -e`)\n\n## Audit Log\n\nAll inference requests through the gateway are logged to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log`. Each line includes timestamp, method, host, and path. The log is readable in `cymclaw status` and the web UI.\n\n## Reporting Issues\n\nFile security issues at: https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw/security/advisories\n","numLines":59,"startLine":1,"totalLines":59}}}

It is inspired by NVIDIA NemoClaw but replaces OpenShift with pure Docker for easier local deployment.\n\n> **Alpha software** — interfaces and behavior may change without notice.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\ncurl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cympotek/CymClaw/main/install.sh | bash\n```\n\nOr from source:\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/cympotek/CymClaw.git\ncd CymClaw\nbash install.sh\n```\n\nAfter install:\n\n```bash\ncymclaw start      # start sandbox + gateway\ncymclaw connect    # shell into sandbox\n```\n\nInside the sandbox:\n\n```bash\nopenclaw tui       # interactive chat with the agent\n```\n\n---\n\n## How It Works\n\n```\ncymclaw start\n    │\n    ├── docker run cymclaw-sandbox\n    │       (--network cymclaw-isolated  ← no direct internet)\n    │       (--read-only --seccomp       ← hardened filesystem)\n    │       OpenClaw agent inside\n    │       OPENAI_BASE_URL → http://cymclaw-gateway:8899/v1\n    │\n    └── node gateway/server.js  (runs on HOST, port 8899)\n            │\n            ├── ① whitelist check   → 403 if host not allowed\n            ├── ② inject Gemini key → Bearer token added\n            └── ③ forward request   → https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/\n```\n\nAll inference calls from the sandbox go through the host-side gateway, which:\n1. Checks the request host against the configurable whitelist\n2. Injects the Gemini API key (the sandbox never sees the real key)\n3. Forwards to Gemini's OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n4. Logs all requests to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log`\n\nThe sandbox container never has direct internet access (Docker `--internal` network).\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n| Resource | Minimum | Recommended |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| CPU      | 2 vCPU  | 4+ vCPU     |\n| RAM      | 4 GB    | 8 GB        |\n| Disk     | 5 GB    | 10 GB       |\n\n| Dependency | Version |\n|------------|---------|\n| Docker     | 24+     |\n| Node.js    | 22+     |\n| pnpm       | 9+      |\n\nSupported platforms: Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel).\n\n---\n\n## Security Layers\n\n| Layer      | Mechanism                                          | Notes                              |\n|------------|----------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------|\n| Network    | Docker `--internal` bridge + gateway proxy         | iptables rules on Linux too        |\n| Filesystem | `--read-only` + `--tmpfs /tmp`                     | Write only to `/sandbox/workspace` |\n| Process    | `--security-opt no-new-privileges`                 | No privilege escalation            |\n| Syscalls   | `--security-opt seccomp=cymclaw-seccomp.json`       | Blocks 40+ dangerous syscalls      |\n| User       | Non-root `sandbox` user (UID dynamic)              | Limits container breakout damage   |\n| Inference  | Gateway proxy intercepts all AI calls              | Whitelist + key injection          |\n\nSee [docs/security.md](docs/security.md) for full details.\n\n---\n\n## CymClaw vs Alternatives\n\n| Feature                  | **CymClaw**          | NemoClaw              | Raw Docker           |\n|--------------------------|----------------------|-----------------------|----------------------|\n| Setup complexity         | One-command install  | Requires OpenShift    | Manual configuration |\n| API key exposure         | Key never in sandbox | Varies                | Full exposure        |\n| Network whitelist        | Built-in, editable   | Fixed                 | None                 |\n| Audit logging            | Always-on            | Limited               | None                 |\n| Web UI                   | Included             | None                  | None                 |\n| Seccomp profile          | Included             | Partial               | Default Docker       |\n| Platform support         | Linux + macOS        | Linux only            | Any                  |\n\n---\n\n## CLI Reference\n\n```\ncymclaw install          First-time setup wizard\ncymclaw start            Start sandbox + gateway\ncymclaw stop             Stop everything\ncymclaw connect          Open shell inside sandbox\ncymclaw status           Show running status\ncymclaw logs [-f]        Stream sandbox logs\ncymclaw config           Edit config interactively\ncymclaw ui               Open web config UI (localhost:3847)\ncymclaw policy add <host>    Add host to network whitelist\ncymclaw policy list          List allowed endpoints\ncymclaw policy remove <host> Remove from whitelist\ncymclaw doctor           Check Docker, Node, network health\ncymclaw update           Self-update from GitHub\ncymclaw uninstall        Remove CymClaw\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nConfig is stored at `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600).\n\n| Key | Default | Description |\n|-----|---------|-------------|\n| `geminiApiKey` | — | Google AI API key |\n| `model` | `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` | Gemini model |\n| `gatewayPort` | `8899` | Gateway listen port |\n| `uiPort` | `3847` | Web UI port |\n| `networkWhitelist` | see defaults | Allowed outbound hosts |\n| `logAudit` | `true` | Log requests to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log` |\n\nGet a free Gemini API key at [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey).\n\nEnvironment variables (for `.env` or CI):\n\n```\nGEMINI_API_KEY=your-key\nCYMCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=8899\nCYMCLAW_UI_PORT=3847\n```\n\n---\n\n## Web UI\n\n```bash\ncymclaw ui   # opens http://localhost:3847\n```\n\nFeatures:\n- View sandbox + gateway status (real-time)\n- Edit Gemini API key and model\n- Manage network whitelist (add/remove hosts)\n- View audit log of recent inference calls\n- Start/stop sandbox\n- Dark/light mode toggle\n\n---\n\n## Network Whitelist Presets\n\n| Preset | Included hosts |\n|--------|---------------|\n| `default` | Gemini, Anthropic, OpenClaw, GitHub, npm |\n| `minimal` | Gemini only |\n| `development` | default + PyPI, HuggingFace, Docker Hub |\n| `cympack` | default + Jina AI, Pacdora |\n| `slack` | default + Slack API |\n| `telegram` | default + Telegram Bot API |\n\n---\n\n## FAQ\n\n**Q: Does the sandbox have internet access?**  \nA: Only to hosts on the whitelist, via the gateway proxy. The Docker `--internal` network blocks all other outbound traffic.\n\n**Q: Is my Gemini API key safe?**  \nA: The key is stored in `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600) and injected into the gateway at startup. The sandbox container never receives the real key directly.\n\n**Q: Can the agent escape the sandbox?**  \nA: CymClaw uses Docker's `--read-only` filesystem, seccomp syscall filtering, `no-new-privileges`, and a non-root user. Container escape is not impossible but requires chaining multiple vulnerabilities. See [docs/security.md](docs/security.md).\n\n**Q: What models are supported?**  \nA: Any Gemini model via `cymclaw config`. Tested with `gemini-2.0-flash-exp`, `gemini-1.5-flash`, and `gemini-1.5-pro`.\n\n**Q: Does it work on Windows?**  \nA: Not currently. WSL2 may work but is untested. Pull requests welcome.\n\n**Q: How do I add a custom tool to the agent?**  \nA: Place tools in `/sandbox/workspace` and configure them in OpenClaw's agent definition. See [OpenClaw docs](https://openclaw.ai).\n\n---\n\n## Learn More\n\n- [Quickstart](docs/quickstart.md)\n- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)\n- [Security](docs/security.md)\n- [Configuration](docs/configuration.md)\n- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n\n## License\n\nApache License 2.0 — see [LICENSE](LICENSE).\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 2: Fix package.json\n\nReplace the entire gateway status, and better UX:\n\n```javascript\n// CymClaw Config UI\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\n'use strict';\n\n// ── State ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nlet config = {};\nlet statusInterval = null;\n\n// ── Theme ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction applyTheme(theme) {\n  document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', theme);\n  localStorage.setItem('cymclaw-theme', theme);\n  document.getElementById('btn-theme').textContent = theme === 'dark' ? '☀' : '🌙';\n}\n\ndocument.getElementById('btn-theme').addEventListener('click', () => {\n  const current = document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') || 'light';\n  applyTheme(current === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark');\n});\n\n// Restore saved theme\n(function () {\n  const saved = localStorage.getItem('cymclaw-theme') || 'light';\n  applyTheme(saved);\n})();\n\n// ── API helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\nasync function apiGet(path) {\n  const r = await fetch(path);\n  if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${r.status}`);\n  return r.json();\n}\n\nasync function apiPost(path, body) {\n  const r = await fetch(path, {\n    method: 'POST',\n    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n    body: JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n  if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${r.status}`);\n  return r.json();\n}\n\n// ── Status ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nasync function refreshStatus() {\n  try {\n    const s = await apiGet('/api/status');\n    const badge = document.getElementById('status-badge');\n\n    const sandboxDot = document.getElementById('sandbox-dot');\n    const sandboxVal = document.getElementById('sandbox-status');\n    sandboxDot.className = 'status-dot ' + (s.sandboxRunning ? 'ok' : 'error');\n    sandboxVal.textContent = s.sandboxRunning ? 'running' : 'stopped';\n\n    const gatewayDot = document.getElementById('gateway-dot');\n    const gatewayVal = document.getElementById('gateway-status');\n    gatewayDot.className = 'status-dot ' + (s.gatewayRunning ? 'ok' : 'error');\n    gatewayVal.textContent = s.gatewayRunning ? 'running' : 'stopped';\n\n    const allOk = s.sandboxRunning && s.gatewayRunning;\n    badge.className = 'badge ' + (allOk ? 'badge-ok' : s.sandboxRunning || s.gatewayRunning ? 'badge-warn' : 'badge-error');\n    badge.textContent = allOk ? 'running' : s.sandboxRunning || s.gatewayRunning ? 'partial' : 'stopped';\n\n    // Audit log\n    if (s.recentLogs && s.recentLogs.length > 0) {\n      const el = document.getElementById('audit-log');\n      el.innerHTML = s.recentLogs.map((line) => {\n        const cls = line.includes('ALLOW') ? 'allow'\n                  : line.includes('BLOCK') ? 'block'\n                  : line.includes('ERROR') ? 'error-line' : '';\n        return `<p class=\"${cls}\">${escHtml(line)}</p>`;\n      }).join('');\n      el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;\n    }\n  } catch (err) {\n    document.getElementById('status-badge').className = 'badge badge-error';\n    document.getElementById('status-badge').textContent = 'error';\n  }\n}\n\n// ── Config form ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\nasync function loadConfig() {\n  config = await apiGet('/api/config');\n  populateForm(config);\n}\n\nfunction populateForm(cfg) {\n  document.getElementById('geminiApiKey').value = cfg.geminiApiKey || '';\n  document.getElementById('model').value       = cfg.model || 'gemini-2.0-flash-exp';\n  document.getElementById('logAudit').checked  = !!cfg.logAudit;\n  document.getElementById('gatewayPort').value = cfg.gatewayPort || 8899;\n  renderWhitelist(cfg.networkWhitelist || []);\n}\n\ndocument.getElementById('config-form').addEventListener('submit', async (e) => {\n  e.preventDefault();\n  const data = {\n    geminiApiKey: document.getElementById('geminiApiKey').value.trim(),\n    model:        document.getElementById('model').value,\n    logAudit:     document.getElementById('logAudit').checked,\n    gatewayPort:  parseInt(document.getElementById('gatewayPort').value, 10),\n    networkWhitelist: config.networkWhitelist || [],\n  };\n  try {\n    await apiPost('/api/config', data);\n    config = { ...config, ...data };\n    const msg = document.getElementById('save-status');\n    msg.textContent = '✓ Saved';\n    setTimeout(() => (msg.textContent = ''), 2000);\n  } catch {\n    alert('Failed to save config.');\n  }\n});\n\n// ── Whitelist ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction renderWhitelist(list) {\n  const ul = document.getElementById('whitelist');\n  ul.innerHTML = '';\n  list.forEach((host) => {\n    const li = document.createElement('li');\n    li.innerHTML = `<span>${escHtml(host)}</span>\n      <button class=\"remove-host\" title=\"Remove\" data-host=\"${escHtml(host)}\">×</button>`;\n    ul.appendChild(li);\n  });\n  ul.querySelectorAll('.remove-host').forEach((btn) => {\n    btn.addEventListener('click', () => removeHost(btn.dataset.host));\n  });\n}\n\nfunction removeHost(host) {\n  config.networkWhitelist = (config.networkWhitelist || []).filter((h) => h !== host);\n  renderWhitelist(config.networkWhitelist);\n  saveWhitelist();\n}\n\ndocument.getElementById('btn-add-host').addEventListener('click', () => {\n  const input = document.getElementById('new-host');\n  const host = input.value.trim();\n  if (!host) return;\n  if ((config.networkWhitelist || []).includes(host)) {\n    input.value = '';\n    return;\n  }\n  config.networkWhitelist = [...(config.networkWhitelist || []), host];\n  renderWhitelist(config.networkWhitelist);\n  saveWhitelist();\n  input.value = '';\n});\n\ndocument.getElementById('new-host').addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n  if (e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); document.getElementById('btn-add-host').click(); }\n});\n\nasync function saveWhitelist() {\n  try {\n    await apiPost('/api/config', { networkWhitelist: config.networkWhitelist });\n  } catch {}\n}\n\n// ── Sandbox controls ───────────────────────────────────────────────\ndocument.getElementById('btn-start').addEventListener('click', async () => {\n  const btn = document.getElementById('btn-start');\n  btn.disabled = true;\n  btn.textContent = 'Starting...';\n  try {\n    await apiPost('/api/sandbox/start', {});\n  } catch {}\n  setTimeout(() => {\n    btn.disabled = false;\n    btn.textContent = '▶ Start';\n    refreshStatus();\n  }, 3000);\n});\n\ndocument.getElementById('btn-stop').addEventListener('click', async () => {\n  if (!confirm('Stop the CymClaw sandbox?')) return;\n  const btn = document.getElementById('btn-stop');\n  btn.disabled = true;\n  btn.textContent = 'Stopping...';\n  try {\n    await apiPost('/api/sandbox/stop', {});\n  } catch {}\n  setTimeout(() => {\n    btn.disabled = false;\n    btn.textContent = '■ Stop';\n    refreshStatus();\n  }, 2000);\n});\n\ndocument.getElementById('btn-refresh').addEventListener('click', refreshStatus);\n\n// ── Audit log clear ───────────────────────────────────────────────\ndocument.getElementById('btn-clear-audit').addEventListener('click', () => {\n  document.getElementById('audit-log').innerHTML = '';\n});\n\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction escHtml(s) {\n  return String(s)\n    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\n    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\n    .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;');\n}\n\n// ── Init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n(async () => {\n  await loadConfig();\n  await refreshStatus();\n  statusInterval = setInterval(refreshStatus, 5000);\n})();\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 6: Update ui/style.css\n\nReplace the entire /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/ui/style.css with this version that adds dark mode support
