none;\n  margin-bottom: 10px;\n}\n.whitelist-list li {\n  display: flex;\n  align-items: center;\n  justify-content: space-between;\n  padding: 6px 10px;\n  background: var(--bg-input);\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  margin-bottom: 5px;\n  font-size: 13px;\n  font-family: monospace;\n}\n.whitelist-list li .remove-host {\n  background: none;\n  border: none;\n  color: var(--gray-400);\n  cursor: pointer;\n  font-size: 15px;\n  line-height: 1;\n  padding: 0 4px;\n}\n.whitelist-list li .remove-host:hover { color: var(--red); }\n\n.add-host-row {\n  display: flex;\n  gap: 8px;\n}\n.add-host-row input {\n  flex: 1;\n  padding: 7px 10px;\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  font-size: 13px;\n  font-family: monospace;\n  background: var(--bg-input);\n  color: var(--text);\n}\n.add-host-row input:focus { outline: none; border-color: var(--blue); }\n\n/* Audit log */\n.audit-log {\n  background: #0f172a;\n  color: #94a3b8;\n  font-family: 'Menlo', 'Monaco', 'Courier New', monospace;\n  font-size: 11px;\n  line-height: 1.6;\n  padding: 12px 14px;\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  max-height: 220px;\n  overflow-y: auto;\n  margin-bottom: 8px;\n}\n.audit-log .allow { color: #4ade80; }\n.audit-log .block { color: #f87171; }\n.audit-log .error-line { color: #fb923c; }\n.audit-log p { margin: 0; }\n\n/* Footer */\nfooter {\n  text-align: center;\n  padding: 16px;\n  color: var(--gray-400);\n  font-size: 12px;\n}\nfooter a { color: var(--blue); text-decoration: none; }\n\n/* Mobile responsiveness */\n@media (max-width: 600px) {\n  header { padding: 10px 16px; }\n  .brand-tagline { display: none; }\n  main { margin: 12px auto; padding: 0 10px; gap: 12px; }\n  .card { padding: 14px 16px; }\n  .status-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }\n  .action-row { flex-direction: column; align-items: stretch; }\n  .action-row .btn { text-align: center; }\n  .add-host-row { flex-direction: column; }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 7: Update bin/lib/ui.js\n\nReplace the entire /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/bin/lib/ui.js with this version that adds gateway status, sandbox start/stop endpoints, and CORS:\n\n```javascript\n// CymClaw — web UI launcher\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\n'use strict';\n\nconst http   = require('http');\nconst fs     = require('fs');\nconst path   = require('path');\nconst { execSync, spawnSync } = require('child_process');\nconst { loadConfig, saveConfig, CONFIG_DIR } = require('./config');\nconst { ROOT } = require('./runner');\nconst { UI_PORT, GATEWAY_PORT } = require('./constants');\n\nconst CORS_HEADERS = {\n  'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'http://localhost:' + (process.env.CYMCLAW_UI_PORT || UI_PORT),\n  'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',\n  'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type',\n};\n\nfunction setCors(res) {\n  Object.entries(CORS_HEADERS).forEach(([k, v]) => res.setHeader(k, v));\n}\n\nfunction readBody(req) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let body = '';\n    req.on('data', (c) => (body += c));\n    req.on('end', () => resolve(body));\n  });\n}\n\nfunction isGatewayRunning(port) {\n  try {\n    const http = require('http');\n    return new Promise((resolve) => {\n      const req = http.get(`http://127.0.0.1:${port}/health`, (res) => {\n        resolve(res.statusCode === 200);\n        res.resume();\n      });\n      req.on('error', () => resolve(false));\n      req.setTimeout(500, () => { req.destroy(); resolve(false); });\n    });\n  } catch {\n    return Promise.resolve(false);\n  }\n}\n\nasync function launchUi() {\n  const cfg = loadConfig();\n  const port = cfg.uiPort || UI_PORT;\n  const uiDir = path.join(ROOT, 'ui');\n\n  const server = http.createServer(async (req, res) => {\n    setCors(res);\n\n    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {\n      res.writeHead(204); res.end(); return;\n    }\n\n    // ── GET /api/config ───────────────────────────────────────────\n    if (req.method === 'GET' && req.url === '/api/config') {\n      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n      res.end(JSON.stringify(loadConfig()));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // ── POST /api/config ──────────────────────────────────────────\n    if (req.method === 'POST' && req.url === '/api/config') {\n      const body = await readBody(req);\n      try {\n        const incoming = JSON.parse(body);\n        const current = loadConfig();\n        saveConfig({ ...current, ...incoming });\n        res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n        res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }));\n      } catch {\n        res.writeHead(400); res.end('Bad Request');\n      }\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // ── GET /api/status ───────────────────────────────────────────\n    if (req.method === 'GET' && req.url === '/api/status') {\n      const { dockerPs } = require('./docker');\n      const { SANDBOX_NAME } = require('./constants');\n      const currentCfg = loadConfig();\n      const gPort = currentCfg.gatewayPort || GATEWAY_PORT;\n\n      const sandboxRunning = dockerPs(SANDBOX_NAME);\n      const gatewayRunning = await isGatewayRunning(gPort);\n\n      const auditPath = path.join(CONFIG_DIR, 'audit.log');\n      let recentLogs = [];\n      if (fs.existsSync(auditPath)) {\n        recentLogs = fs.readFileSync(auditPath, 'utf-8').trim().split('\\n').filter(Boolean).slice(-20);\n      }\n      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n      res.end(JSON.stringify({ sandboxRunning, gatewayRunning, recentLogs }));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // ── POST /api/sandbox/start ───────────────────────────────────\n    if (req.method === 'POST' && req.url === '/api/sandbox/start') {\n      try {\n        spawnSync('cymclaw', ['start'], { stdio: 'ignore', detached: true });\n      } catch {}\n      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n      res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // ── POST /api/sandbox/stop ────────────────────────────────────\n    if (req.method === 'POST' && req.url === '/api/sandbox/stop') {\n      try {\n        spawnSync('cymclaw', ['stop'], { stdio: 'ignore' });\n      } catch {}\n      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n      res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true }));\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // ── Static files ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n    let filePath = req.url === '/' ? '/index.html' : req.url;\n    filePath = path.join(uiDir, filePath);\n    if (!filePath.startsWith(uiDir)) { res.writeHead(403); res.end(); return; }\n    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) { res.writeHead(404); res.end('Not found'); return; }\n\n    const ext = path.extname(filePath);\n    const types = { '.html': 'text/html', '.js': 'application/javascript', '.css': 'text/css' };\n    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': types[ext] || 'text/plain' });\n    fs.createReadStream(filePath).pipe(res);\n  });\n\n  server.listen(port, '127.0.0.1', () => {\n    const url = `http://localhost:${port}`;\n    console.log(`  \\x1b[32m✓\\x1b[0m CymClaw UI running at \\x1b[34m${url}\\x1b[0m`);\n    console.log('  Press Ctrl+C to stop the UI server.');\n    try {\n      const opener = process.platform === 'darwin' ? 'open' : 'xdg-open';\n      execSync(`${opener} ${url}`, { stdio: 'ignore' });\n    } catch {}\n  });\n\n  await new Promise(() => {});\n}\n\nmodule.exports = { launchUi };\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 8: Update gateway/server.js\n\nReplace the entire /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/gateway/server.js with this improved version adding rate limiting and CORS:\n\n```javascript\n#!/usr/bin/env node\n// CymClaw inference gateway — HTTP proxy that routes AI calls to Gemini\n// Runs on the HOST, not inside Docker.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\n'use strict';\n\nconst http   = require('http');\nconst https  = require('https');\nconst url    = require('url');\nconst fs     = require('fs');\nconst path   = require('path');\n\n// ── Config from environment ────────────────────────────────────────\nconst GEMINI_API_KEY = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY || '';\nconst GEMINI_MODEL   = process.env.GEMINI_MODEL   || 'gemini-2.0-flash-exp';\nconst GATEWAY_PORT   = parseInt(process.env.GATEWAY_PORT || '8899', 10);\nconst AUDIT_LOG      = process.env.AUDIT_LOG === '1';\nconst AUDIT_LOG_PATH = process.env.AUDIT_LOG_PATH || path.join(process.env.HOME || '/tmp', '.cymclaw', 'audit.log');\nconst WHITELIST_RAW  = process.env.WHITELIST || '';\n\n// Rate limiting: max requests per window per client IP\nconst RATE_LIMIT_MAX    = parseInt(process.env.RATE_LIMIT_MAX || '60', 10);\nconst RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW = parseInt(process.env.RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW || '60000', 10); // ms\n\n// UI port for CORS\nconst UI_PORT = parseInt(process.env.UI_PORT || '3847', 10);\n\n// Gemini OpenAI-compatible endpoint\nconst GEMINI_UPSTREAM = {\n  host: 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',\n  basePath: '/v1beta/openai',\n  port: 443,\n};\n\n// Build whitelist set — always include Gemini\nconst WHITELIST = new Set([\n  'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',\n  ...(WHITELIST_RAW ? WHITELIST_RAW.split(',').map((h) => h.trim()).filter(Boolean) : []),\n]);\n\n// ── Rate limiting ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\nconst rateLimitMap = new Map(); // ip → { count, resetAt }\n\nfunction checkRateLimit(ip) {\n  const now = Date.now();\n  let entry = rateLimitMap.get(ip);\n  if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {\n    entry = { count: 0, resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW };\n    rateLimitMap.set(ip, entry);\n  }\n  entry.count += 1;\n  return entry.count <= RATE_LIMIT_MAX;\n}\n\n// Clean up stale entries periodically\nsetInterval(() => {\n  const now = Date.now();\n  for (const [ip, entry] of rateLimitMap) {\n    if (now > entry.resetAt) rateLimitMap.delete(ip);\n  }\n}, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW);\n\n// ── CORS headers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction setCorsHeaders(res) {\n  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', `http://localhost:${UI_PORT}`);\n  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Methods', 'GET, POST, OPTIONS');\n  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Headers', 'Content-Type, Authorization, x-cymclaw-target');\n}\n\n// ── Audit logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction auditLog(entry) {\n  if (!AUDIT_LOG) return;\n  const line = `[${new Date().toISOString()}] ${entry}\\n`;\n  try {\n    fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(AUDIT_LOG_PATH), { recursive: true });\n    fs.appendFileSync(AUDIT_LOG_PATH, line);\n  } catch {}\n}\n\n// ── Request helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction readBody(req) {\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    const chunks = [];\n    req.on('data', (c) => chunks.push(c));\n    req.on('end', () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n    req.on('error', reject);\n  });\n}\n\nfunction isWhitelisted(targetHost) {\n  if (WHITELIST.has(targetHost)) return true;\n  // Check suffix match (e.g. *.googleapis.com)\n  for (const h of WHITELIST) {\n    if (h.startsWith('*.') && targetHost.endsWith(h.slice(1))) return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\n// ── Health check ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction handleHealth(req, res) {\n  setCorsHeaders(res);\n  res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n  res.end(JSON.stringify({\n    status: 'ok',\n    model: GEMINI_MODEL,\n    whitelist: [...WHITELIST],\n    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    uptime: process.uptime(),\n  }));\n}\n\n// ── Proxy to Gemini ────────────────────────────────────────────────\nasync function proxyToGemini(req, res, body) {\n  const parsedUrl = url.parse(req.url);\n  let targetPath = parsedUrl.pathname || '/';\n  if (targetPath.startsWith('/v1')) {\n    targetPath = GEMINI_UPSTREAM.basePath + targetPath.slice(3);\n  } else {\n    targetPath = GEMINI_UPSTREAM.basePath + targetPath;\n  }\n  if (parsedUrl.query) targetPath += '?' + parsedUrl.query;\n\n  const upstreamHeaders = {\n    ...req.headers,\n    host: GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host,\n    authorization: `Bearer ${GEMINI_API_KEY}`,\n    'content-length': body.length,\n  };\n  ['connection', 'keep-alive', 'proxy-authenticate', 'proxy-authorization',\n   'te', 'trailers', 'transfer-encoding', 'upgrade'].forEach((h) => delete upstreamHeaders[h]);\n\n  auditLog(`ALLOW ${req.method} ${GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host}${targetPath} (${body.length}B)`);\n\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    const upstreamReq = https.request({\n      hostname: GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host,\n      port: GEMINI_UPSTREAM.port,\n      path: targetPath,\n      method: req.method,\n      headers: upstreamHeaders,\n    }, (upstreamRes) => {\n      setCorsHeaders(res);\n      res.writeHead(upstreamRes.statusCode, upstreamRes.headers);\n      upstreamRes.pipe(res);\n      upstreamRes.on('end', resolve);\n      upstreamRes.on('error', (err) => {\n        console.error(`[gateway] upstream response error: ${err.message}`);\n        resolve();\n      });\n    });\n\n    upstreamReq.on('error', (err) => {\n      console.error(`[gateway] upstream error: ${err.message}`);\n      auditLog(`ERROR upstream: ${err.message}`);\n      if (!res.headersSent) {\n        setCorsHeaders(res);\n        res.writeHead(502, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n        res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: { message: 'Gateway upstream error: ' + err.message, type: 'proxy_error' } }));\n      }\n      resolve();\n    });\n\n    upstreamReq.setTimeout(30000, () => {\n      upstreamReq.destroy(new Error('upstream request timeout'));\n    });\n\n    upstreamReq.write(body);\n    upstreamReq.end();\n  });\n}\n\n// ── Proxy to arbitrary whitelisted host ────────────────────────────\nasync function proxyToHost(req, res, body, targetHost) {\n  const parsedUrl = url.parse(req.url);\n  const targetPath = parsedUrl.path || '/';\n\n  const upstreamHeaders = { ...req.headers, host: targetHost };\n  ['connection', 'keep-alive', 'transfer-encoding'].forEach((h) => delete upstreamHeaders[h]);\n  if (body.length > 0) upstreamHeaders['content-length'] = body.length;\n\n  auditLog(`ALLOW ${req.method} ${targetHost}${targetPath} (${body.length}B)`);\n\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    const upstreamReq = https.request({\n      hostname: targetHost,\n      port: 443,\n      path: targetPath,\n      method: req.method,\n      headers: upstreamHeaders,\n    }, (upstreamRes) => {\n      setCorsHeaders(res);\n      res.writeHead(upstreamRes.statusCode, upstreamRes.headers);\n      upstreamRes.pipe(res);\n      upstreamRes.on('end', resolve);\n    });\n\n    upstreamReq.on('error', (err) => {\n      if (!res.headersSent) {\n        setCorsHeaders(res);\n        res.writeHead(502); res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }));\n      }\n      resolve();\n    });\n\n    upstreamReq.setTimeout(30000, () => {\n      upstreamReq.destroy(new Error('upstream request timeout'));\n    });\n\n    if (body.length > 0) upstreamReq.write(body);\n    upstreamReq.end();\n  });\n}\n\n// ── Main handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\nasync function handleRequest(req, res) {\n  // CORS preflight\n  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {\n    setCorsHeaders(res);\n    res.writeHead(204); res.end(); return;\n  }\n\n  if (req.url === '/health' || req.url === '/healthz') {\n    handleHealth(req, res); return;\n  }\n\n  // Rate limiting (skip for sandbox containers on private subnets)\n  const clientIp = req.socket.remoteAddress || '';\n  const isPrivate = clientIp.startsWith('172.') || clientIp.startsWith('10.') || clientIp === '127.0.0.1' || clientIp === '::1';\n  if (!isPrivate && !checkRateLimit(clientIp)) {\n    auditLog(`RATE_LIMIT ${clientIp} ${req.method} ${req.url}`);\n    setCorsHeaders(res);\n    res.writeHead(429, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n    res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: { message: 'Rate limit exceeded', type: 'rate_limit', code: 429 } }));\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const body = await readBody(req);\n\n  // Determine target host from header or URL\n  const targetHost = req.headers['x-cymclaw-target'] || GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host;\n\n  if (!isWhitelisted(targetHost)) {\n    auditLog(`BLOCK ${req.method} ${targetHost}${req.url}`);\n    console.log(`[gateway] BLOCKED: ${targetHost}`);\n    setCorsHeaders(res);\n    res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n    res.end(JSON.stringify({\n      error: {\n        message: `CymClaw gateway: host '${targetHost}' is not whitelisted`,\n        type: 'policy_violation',\n        code: 403,\n      },\n    }));\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Route AI calls to Gemini (OpenAI-compat path)\n  if (req.url.startsWith('/v1/') || req.url.startsWith('/v1beta/') ||\n      targetHost === GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host) {\n    await proxyToGemini(req, res, body);\n    return;\n  }\n\n  await proxyToHost(req, res, body, targetHost);\n}\n\n// ── Server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nconst server = http.createServer((req, res) => {\n  handleRequest(req, res).catch((err) => {\n    console.error(`[gateway] unhandled error: ${err.message}`);\n    if (!res.headersSent) {\n      setCorsHeaders(res);\n      res.writeHead(500); res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: 'internal gateway error' }));\n    }\n  });\n});\n\nserver.listen(GATEWAY_PORT, '0.0.0.0', () => {\n  console.log(`[cymclaw-gateway] listening on :${GATEWAY_PORT}`);\n  console.log(`[cymclaw-gateway] model: ${GEMINI_MODEL}`);\n  console.log(`[cymclaw-gateway] whitelist: ${[...WHITELIST].join(', ')}`);\n  if (!GEMINI_API_KEY) {\n    console.warn('[cymclaw-gateway] WARNING: GEMINI_API_KEY is not set');\n  }\n});\n\nserver.on('error', (err) => {\n  console.error(`[cymclaw-gateway] server error: ${err.message}`);\n  process.exit(1);\n});\n\nprocess.on('SIGTERM', () => { server.close(() => process.exit(0)); });\nprocess.on('SIGINT',  () => { server.close(() => process.exit(0)); });\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 9: Update bin/cymclaw.js\n\nReplace the entire /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/bin/cymclaw.js with this improved version adding `doctor` and `update` commands:\n\n```javascript\n#!/usr/bin/env node\n// CymClaw CLI — main entry point\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\n'use strict';\n\nconst { spawnSync, execSync } = require('child_process');\nconst path = require('path');\nconst fs = require('fs');\nconst os = require('os');\n\nconst ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, '..');\nconst { run, runInteractive, runCapture } = require('./lib/runner');\nconst { loadConfig, saveConfig, CONFIG_PATH } = require('./lib/config');\nconst { dockerPs, dockerExec, dockerLogs, dockerStop, dockerRm } = require('./lib/docker');\nconst { SANDBOX_NAME, GATEWAY_NAME, GATEWAY_PORT, UI_PORT } = require('./lib/constants');\n\n// ── ASCII banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction banner() {\n  console.log('\\x1b[34m');\n  console.log('   ██████╗██╗   ██╗███╗   ███╗ ██████╗██╗      █████╗ ██╗    ██╗');\n  console.log('  ██╔════╝╚██╗ ██╔╝████╗ ████║██╔════╝██║     ██╔══██╗██║    ██║');\n  console.log('  ██║      ╚████╔╝ ██╔████╔██║██║     ██║     ███████║██║ █╗ ██║');\n  console.log('  ██║       ╚██╔╝  ██║╚██╔╝██║██║     ██║     ██╔══██║██║███╗██║');\n  console.log('  ╚██████╗   ██║   ██║ ╚═╝ ██║╚██████╗███████╗██║  ██║╚███╔███╔╝');\n  console.log('   ╚═════╝   ╚═╝   ╚═╝     ╚═╝ ╚═════╝╚══════╝╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚══╝╚══╝ ');\n  console.log('\\x1b[0m');\n  console.log('  \\x1b[2mOpenClaw in Docker · Gemini Flash · by CymPotek\\x1b[0m');\n  console.log('');\n}\n\n// ── Help ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfunction help() {\n  banner();\n  console.log(`  \\x1b[1mUsage:\\x1b[0m cymclaw <command> [options]\n\n  \\x1b[1mSetup:\\x1b[0m\n    install              First-time setup wizard\n    uninstall            Remove CymClaw and all containers\n\n  \\x1b[1mSandbox:\\x1b[0m\n    start                Start sandbox + gateway\n    stop                 Stop everything\n    connect              Open shell inside sandbox\n    status               Show running status\n    logs [-f]            Stream sandbox logs\n\n  \\x1b[1mConfig:\\x1b[0m\n    config               Edit config interactively\n    ui                   Open web config UI (localhost:${UI_PORT})\n\n  \\x1b[1mPolicy:\\x1b[0m\n    policy add <host>    Add host to network whitelist\n    policy list          List allowed endpoints\n    policy remove <host> Remove host from whitelist\n\n  \\x1b[1mMaintenance:\\x1b[0m\n    doctor               Check Docker, Node.js, network health\n    update               Self-update CymClaw from GitHub\n\n  \\x1b[1mOptions:\\x1b[0m\n    --help, -h           Show this help\n    --version, -v        Show version\n`);\n}\n\n// ── Commands ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nasync function cmdInstall(args) {\n  const { runInstall } = require('./lib/install');\n  await runInstall({ nonInteractive: args.includes('--non-interactive') });\n}\n\nasync function cmdStart() {\n  const { startSandbox } = require('./lib/start');\n  await startSandbox();\n}\n\nfunction cmdStop() {\n  console.log('  Stopping CymClaw sandbox...');\n  dockerStop(SANDBOX_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n  dockerRm(SANDBOX_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n  dockerStop(GATEWAY_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n  dockerRm(GATEWAY_NAME, { ignoreError: true });\n  // Also kill gateway process if running by PID\n  const pidFile = path.join(os.homedir(), '.cymclaw', 'gateway.pid');\n  if (fs.existsSync(pidFile)) {\n    try {\n      const pid = parseInt(fs.readFileSync(pidFile, 'utf-8').trim(), 10);\n      if (pid && pid > 0) {\n        try { process.kill(pid, 'SIGTERM'); } catch {}\n      }\n      fs.unlinkSync(pidFile);\n    } catch {}\n  }\n  console.log('  \\x1b[32m✓\\x1b[0m Stopped.');\n}\n\nfunction cmdConnect() {\n  const running = dockerPs(SANDBOX_NAME);\n  if (!running) {\n    console.error('  \\x1b[31m✗\\x1b[0m Sandbox is not running. Run: cymclaw start');\n    console.error('  \\x1b[2mHint: use \"cymclaw doctor\" to check your setup.\\x1b[0m');\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n  console.log(`  Connecting to sandbox '${SANDBOX_NAME}'...`);\n  console.log('  \\x1b[2mTip: run \"openclaw tui\" inside to chat with the agent.\\x1b[0m');\n  console.log('');\n  runInteractive(`docker exec -it ${SANDBOX_NAME} /bin/bash`);\n}\n\nfunction cmdStatus() {\n  const { showStatus } = require('./lib/status');\n  showStatus();\n}\n\nfunction cmdLogs(args) {\n  const follow = args.includes('-f') || args.includes('--follow');\n  const followFlag = follow ? ' -f' : '';\n  const running = dockerPs(SANDBOX_NAME);\n  if (!running) {\n    console.error(`  \\x1b[31m✗\\x1b[0m Sandbox '${SANDBOX_NAME}' is not running.`);\n    console.error('  \\x1b[2mHint: run \"cymclaw start\" to start the sandbox.\\x1b[0m');\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n  run(`docker logs${followFlag} ${SANDBOX_NAME}`);\n}\n\nasync function cmdConfig() {\n  const { editConfig } = require('./lib/config-wizard');\n  await editConfig();\n}\n\nasync function cmdUi() {\n  const { launchUi } = require('./lib/ui');\n  await launchUi();\n}\n\nasync function cmdPolicy(args) {\n  const { policyAdd, policyList, policyRemove } = require('./lib/policy');\n  const sub = args[0];\n  if (sub === 'add') {\n    const host = args[1];\n    if (!host) { console.error('  Usage: cymclaw policy add <host>'); process.exit(1); }\n    await policyAdd(host);\n  } else if (sub === 'list') {\n    policyList();\n  } else if (sub === 'remove') {\n    const host = args[1];\n    if (!host) { console.error('  Usage: cymclaw policy remove <host>'); process.exit(1); }\n    await policyRemove(host);\n  } else {\n    console.error('  Usage: cymclaw policy <add|list|remove> [host]');\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nfunction cmdDoctor() {\n  console.log('\\n  \\x1b[1mCymClaw Doctor\\x1b[0m — checking your environment\\n');\n  let allOk = true;\n\n  function check(label, fn) {\n    try {\n      const result = fn();\n      if (result === false) {\n        console.log(`  \\x1b[31m✗\\x1b[0m ${label}`);\n        allOk = false;\n      } else {\n        console.log(`  \\x1b[32m✓\\x1b[0m ${label}${result && result !== true ? '  \\x1b[2m' + result + '\\x1b[0m' : ''}`);\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.log(`  \\x1b[31m✗\\x1b[0m ${label}  \\x1b[2m${err.message}\\x1b[0m`);\n      allOk = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check Node.js version\n  check('Node.js version', () => {\n    const v = process.version;\n    const major = parseInt(v.slice(1), 10);\n    if (major < 22) throw new Error(`${v} — need ≥22. Run: nvm install 22`);\n    return v;\n  });\n\n  // Check Docker\n  check('Docker installed', () => {\n    const r = spawnSync('docker', ['--version'], { encoding: 'utf-8' });\n    if (r.status !== 0) throw new Error('docker not found. Install Docker from https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/');\n    return r.stdout.trim();\n  });\n\n  // Check Docker daemon\n  check('Docker daemon running', () => {\n    const r = spawnSync('docker', ['info'], { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' });\n    if (r.status !== 0) throw new Error('Docker daemon not responding. Start Docker Desktop or run: sudo systemctl start docker');\n    return true;\n  });\n\n  // Check pnpm\n  check('pnpm installed', () => {\n    const r = spawnSync('pnpm', ['--version'], { encoding: 'utf-8' });\n    if (r.status !== 0) throw new Error('pnpm not found. Run: npm install -g pnpm');\n    return r.stdout.trim();\n  });\n\n  // Check config\n  check('Config file exists', () => {\n    const cfg = loadConfig();\n    if (!cfg.geminiApiKey) throw new Error('Gemini API key not set. Run: cymclaw config');\n    return CONFIG_PATH;\n  });\n\n  // Check Docker network\n  check('CymClaw Docker network', () => {\n    const { dockerNetworkExists } = require('./lib/docker');\n    const { NETWORK_NAME } = require('./lib/constants');\n    if (!dockerNetworkExists(NETWORK_NAME)) throw new Error(`Network '${NETWORK_NAME}' not found. Run: cymclaw install`);\n    return NETWORK_NAME;\n  });\n\n  // Check sandbox image\n  check('Sandbox Docker image', () => {\n    const { SANDBOX_IMAGE } = require('./lib/constants');\n    const r = spawnSync('docker', ['image', 'inspect', SANDBOX_IMAGE], { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' });\n    if (r.status !== 0) throw new Error(`Image '${SANDBOX_IMAGE}' not found. Run: cymclaw install`);\n    return SANDBOX_IMAGE;\n  });\n\n  console.log('');\n  if (allOk) {\n    console.log('  \\x1b[32m✓ All checks passed.\\x1b[0m\\n');\n  } else {\n    console.log('  \\x1b[33m⚠ Some checks failed. See suggestions above.\\x1b[0m\\n');\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nasync function cmdUpdate() {\n  console.log('\\n  \\x1b[1mUpdating CymClaw\\x1b[0m from GitHub...\\n');\n  const repo = 'https://github.com/cympotek/CymClaw.git';\n\n  // Check if running from a git checkout\n  const gitDir = path.join(ROOT, '.git');\n  if (fs.existsSync(gitDir)) {\n    console.log('  Pulling latest changes from git...');\n    const r = spawnSync('git', ['pull', '--ff-only'], { cwd: ROOT, stdio: 'inherit' });\n    if (r.status !== 0) {\n      console.error('  \\x1b[31m✗\\x1b[0m git pull failed. Resolve conflicts and retry.');\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    console.log('  Installing updated dependencies...');\n    const r2 = spawnSync('pnpm', ['install'], { cwd: ROOT, stdio: 'inherit' });\n    if (r2.status !== 0) {\n      console.error('  \\x1b[31m✗\\x1b[0m pnpm install failed.');\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n  } else {\n    console.log('  Installing latest version from GitHub...');\n    const r = spawnSync('pnpm', ['install', '-g', `git+${repo}`], { stdio: 'inherit' });\n    if (r.status !== 0) {\n      console.error('  \\x1b[31m✗\\x1b[0m Update failed. Try: pnpm install -g git+' + repo);\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n  }\n\n  const pkg = require('../package.json');\n  console.log(`\\n  \\x1b[32m✓\\x1b[0m CymClaw updated to v${pkg.version}\\n`);\n}\n\n// ── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst [cmd, ...args] = process.argv.slice(2);\n\n(async () => {\n  if (!cmd || cmd === 'help' || cmd === '--help' || cmd === '-h') {\n    help();\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (cmd === '--version' || cmd === '-v') {\n    const pkg = require('../package.json');\n    console.log(pkg.version);\n    return;\n  }\n\n  switch (cmd) {\n    case 'install':    await cmdInstall(args); break;\n    case 'uninstall':  run(`bash \"${ROOT}/uninstall.sh\"`); break;\n    case 'start':      await cmdStart(); break;\n    case 'stop':       cmdStop(); break;\n    case 'connect':    cmdConnect(); break;\n    case 'status':     cmdStatus(); break;\n    case 'logs':       cmdLogs(args); break;\n    case 'config':     await cmdConfig(); break;\n    case 'ui':         await cmdUi(); break;\n    case 'policy':     await cmdPolicy(args); break;\n    case 'doctor':     cmdDoctor(); break;\n    case 'update':     await cmdUpdate(); break;\n    default:\n      console.error(`  Unknown command: ${cmd}`);\n      console.error(`  Run 'cymclaw help' for usage.`);\n      process.exit(1);\n  }\n})();\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 10: Update sandbox/Dockerfile\n\nReplace the entire /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/sandbox/Dockerfile:\n\n```dockerfile\n# CymClaw sandbox image\n# OpenClaw pre-installed, Gemini-configured, non-root sandbox user\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\nFROM node:22-slim\n\nENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \\\n    TZ=UTC\n\n# System packages\nRUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \\\n        bash \\\n        ca-certificates \\\n        curl \\\n        git \\\n        iproute2 \\\n        procps \\\n        python3 \\\n        python3-pip \\\n        tini \\\n        tzdata \\\n    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \\\n    && echo $TZ > /etc/timezone \\\n    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*\n\n# Create sandbox user\nRUN groupadd -r sandbox && \\\n    useradd -r -g sandbox -d /sandbox -s /bin/bash sandbox && \\\n    mkdir -p /sandbox/.openclaw /sandbox/workspace && \\\n    chown -R sandbox:sandbox /sandbox\n\n# Install OpenClaw CLI globally\nRUN npm install -g openclaw@2026.3.11 --ignore-scripts\n\n# Copy entrypoint\nCOPY entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh\nRUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh\n\n# Working directory\nWORKDIR /sandbox\nUSER sandbox\n\n# Pre-create OpenClaw directory structure\nRUN mkdir -p \\\n      /sandbox/.openclaw/agents/main/agent \\\n      /sandbox/.openclaw/sessions \\\n    && chmod 700 /sandbox/.openclaw\n\n# Write openclaw.json — configure Gemini via gateway (OpenAI-compat)\n# OPENAI_BASE_URL and OPENAI_API_KEY are injected at runtime via docker run -e\n# The gateway translates /v1/* → Gemini's /v1beta/openai/*\nRUN node -e \" \\\n  const fs = require('fs'); \\\n  const config = { \\\n    agents: { \\\n      defaults: { \\\n        model: { \\\n          primary: 'gemini-2.0-flash-exp' \\\n        } \\\n      } \\\n    }, \\\n    models: { \\\n      mode: 'merge', \\\n      providers: { \\\n        gemini: { \\\n          baseUrl: 'http://cymclaw-gateway:8899/v1', \\\n          apiKey: 'placeholder-injected-at-runtime', \\\n          api: 'openai-completions', \\\n          models: [{ \\\n            id: 'gemini-2.0-flash-exp', \\\n            name: 'Gemini 2.0 Flash', \\\n            reasoning: false, \\\n            input: ['text'], \\\n            cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }, \\\n            contextWindow: 1048576, \\\n            maxTokens: 8192 \\\n          }] \\\n        } \\\n      } \\\n    } \\\n  }; \\\n  const p = '/sandbox/.openclaw/openclaw.json'; \\\n  fs.writeFileSync(p, JSON.stringify(config, null, 2), { mode: 0o600 }); \\\n\"\n\n# Pre-run openclaw doctor to initialize plugin directories\nRUN openclaw doctor --fix > /dev/null 2>&1 || true\n\nHEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \\\n  CMD node -e \"require('fs').accessSync('/sandbox/.openclaw/openclaw.json')\" || exit 1\n\nENTRYPOINT [\"/usr/bin/tini\", \"--\", \"/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh\"]\nCMD [\"bash\"]\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 11: Create .env.example\n\nCreate pull_request:\n    branches: [main]\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    name: Test (${{ matrix.os }})\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}\n    strategy:\n      matrix:\n        os: [ubuntu-22.04, macos-latest]\n\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Set up Node.js 22\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v4\n        with:\n          node-version: '22'\n\n      - name: Set up pnpm\n        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4\n        with:\n          version: '9'\n          run_install: false\n\n      - name: Get pnpm store directory\n        id: pnpm-cache\n        shell: bash\n        run: echo \"STORE_PATH=$(pnpm store path)\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n      - name: Cache pnpm modules\n        uses: actions/cache@v4\n        with:\n          path: ${{ steps.pnpm-cache.outputs.STORE_PATH }}\n          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pnpm-store-${{ hashFiles('**/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}\n          restore-keys: |\n            ${{ runner.os }}-pnpm-store-\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: pnpm install\n\n      - name: Run tests\n        run: pnpm test\n\n      - name: Lint check\n        run: node --check bin/cymclaw.js gateway/server.js bin/lib/*.js ui/app.js\n        continue-on-error: true\n\n  docker-build:\n    name: Docker build\n    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04\n\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Build sandbox image\n        run: docker build sandbox/ -t cymclaw-sandbox:ci --no-cache\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 13: Create CONTRIBUTING.md\n\nCreate /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/CONTRIBUTING.md:\n\n```markdown\n# Contributing to CymClaw\n\nThank you for your interest in contributing! This document describes how to set up a development environment and submit changes.\n\n## Development Setup\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/cympotek/CymClaw.git\ncd CymClaw\npnpm install\npnpm link --global   # makes 'cymclaw' available on PATH from source\n```\n\nRun tests:\n\n```bash\npnpm test\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nbin/           CLI entry point and library modules\n  cymclaw.js   Main dispatcher\n  lib/         Individual command implementations\ngateway/       Host-side inference proxy (gateway/server.js)\nui/            Web config interface (static HTML/JS/CSS)\nsandbox/       Docker image for the agent container\npolicies/      Network whitelist presets and seccomp profile\ndocs/          User documentation\ntest/          Unit tests (Node.js built-in test runner)\nscripts/       Utility scripts (Docker setup, health checks)\n```\n\n## Guidelines\n\n- **No dependencies** unless absolutely necessary. The CLI and gateway are intentionally dependency-light.\n- **Security first.** Any change that weakens the network whitelist, seccomp profile, or key handling requires explicit justification.\n- **Tests required** for new gateway whitelist logic, policy handling, or config behavior.\n- **Apache 2.0** — all new files must include the SPDX header `// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0`.\n\n## Running Tests\n\n```bash\n# All tests\npnpm test\n\n# Individual test files\nnode --test test/docker.test.js\nnode --test test/network.test.js\nnode --test test/gateway.test.js\n```\n\n## Submitting a Pull Request\n\n1. Fork the repo and create a feature branch from `main`.\n2. Make your changes with tests where appropriate.\n3. Run `pnpm test` — all tests must pass.\n4. Open a PR against `main` with a clear description of the change.\n\n## Security Issues\n\nPlease do **not** open public issues for security vulnerabilities. Instead, report them via [GitHub Security Advisories](https://github.com/cympotek/CymClaw/security/advisories).\n\n## Code of Conduct\n\nBe respectful. We follow the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/).\n\n## License\n\nBy contributing, you agree your contributions will be licensed under the Apache License 2.0.\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 14: Update docs/security.md\n\nReplace the entire /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/docs/security.md:\n\n```markdown\n# Security Model\n\nCymClaw uses defense in depth: multiple independent layers that each limit what a compromised agent can do.\n\n## Protection Layers\n\n| Layer | Mechanism | Strength |\n|-------|-----------|----------|\n| Network egress | Docker `--internal` + gateway whitelist | Strong on Linux (iptables); gateway-only on macOS |\n| Filesystem | `--read-only` + tmpfs | Prevents persistent writes outside `/sandbox/workspace` |\n| Process | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` | No privilege escalation |\n| Syscalls | seccomp profile (40+ blocked) | Kernel-level call filtering |\n| User | Non-root `sandbox` user | Limits container breakout damage |\n| Inference | Gateway key injection + audit logging | Agent never holds the real API key |\n\n## Network Whitelist\n\nThe gateway enforces an explicit allowlist. Requests to any unlisted host return HTTP 403.\n\nDefault allowed hosts:\n- `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` — Gemini API (always allowed)\n- `api.anthropic.com` — OpenClaw telemetry\n- `openclaw.ai`, `clawhub.com` — OpenClaw services\n- `github.com`, `api.github.com` — git operations\n- `registry.npmjs.org` — npm package installs\n\nAdd hosts with `cymclaw policy add <host>` or edit `~/.cymclaw/config.json`.\n\n### macOS vs Linux\n\nOn **Linux**, two layers enforce the whitelist:\n1. The gateway proxy (Node.js, user-space)\n2. iptables rules in `CYMCLAW` chain (kernel)\n\nOn **macOS**, Docker Desktop does not expose the host kernel's iptables. The Docker `--internal` network still blocks direct outbound routing, and the gateway proxy is the sole allowed egress path. This provides equivalent security for agent use.\n\n## Seccomp Profile\n\n`policies/cymclaw-seccomp.json` uses an allowlist model (default: deny). It permits ~130 standard POSIX syscalls and blocks dangerous ones including:\n\n| Blocked syscall | Risk prevented |\n|-----------------|----------------|\n| `mount`, `umount2` | Filesystem mounting |\n| `kexec_load`, `kexec_file_load` | Kernel replacement |\n| `init_module`, `finit_module` | Kernel module loading |\n| `reboot` | System reboot |\n| `bpf` | eBPF programs (kernel manipulation) |\n| `iopl`, `ioperm` | Direct hardware I/O |\n| `ptrace` | Process tracing (restricted) |\n| `pivot_root` | Container escape vector |\n| `clone` with `CLONE_NEWUSER` | User namespace privilege escalation |\n| `unshare` with `CLONE_NEWUSER` | Same |\n\n## AppArmor (Linux)\n\nAn AppArmor profile template is provided at `policies/apparmor-cymclaw`. On systems with AppArmor enabled, load it with:\n\n```bash\nsudo apparmor_parser -r policies/apparmor-cymclaw\ndocker run --security-opt apparmor=cymclaw-sandbox ...\n```\n\nThe profile restricts:\n- `/proc` writes (prevents kernel parameter manipulation)\n- `/sys` writes\n- Raw network socket creation\n- Execution of interpreters outside `/sandbox`\n\n## API Key Security\n\nThe Gemini API key is:\n- Stored in `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600, owner-readable only)\n- Passed to the gateway at startup via environment variable\n- Written into `openclaw.json` inside the container at runtime by `entrypoint.sh`\n- **Never exposed to the sandbox as a directly readable variable** after the entrypoint runs\n\n**Known limitation:** The key is visible in `docker inspect` output and in `/proc/<pid>/environ` inside the container while the process runs. This is a fundamental limitation of `docker run -e`. For higher-assurance deployments, consider Docker secrets or a secrets manager.\n\n## Audit Log\n\nAll requests through the gateway are logged to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log`:\n\n```\n[2026-03-15T12:34:56.789Z] ALLOW POST generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/chat/completions (1234B)\n[2026-03-15T12:35:01.123Z] BLOCK GET evil.example.com/exfil\n```\n\nEach line: timestamp, verdict (ALLOW/BLOCK/ERROR), method, host+path, body size.\n\nView recent entries in `cymclaw status` or the web UI.\n\n## Rate Limiting\n\nThe gateway enforces a per-IP rate limit (default: 60 requests/minute for external IPs). Sandbox containers on the internal Docker network (`172.x.x.x`) are exempt.\n\n## Reporting Vulnerabilities\n\nFile security issues at: https://github.com/cympotek/CymClaw/security/advisories\n\nDo **not** open public GitHub issues for security vulnerabilities.\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 15: Create policies/presets/minimal.yaml\n\nCreate /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/policies/presets/minimal.yaml:\n\n```yaml\n# CymClaw — minimal preset\n# Only allows Gemini API. Nothing else.\n# Use this when you want maximum isolation and your agent only needs inference.\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\nname: minimal\ndescription: \"Minimal: Gemini API only — maximum network isolation\"\n\nwhitelist:\n  # Google Generative Language API — required for all Gemini models\n  - generativelanguage.googleapis.com\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 16: Create policies/presets/development.yaml\n\nCreate /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/policies/presets/development.yaml:\n\n```yaml\n# CymClaw — development preset\n# More permissive whitelist for development and testing.\n# WARNING: Do not use this in production or with untrusted agents.\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\nname: development\ndescription: \"Development: default + PyPI + HuggingFace + Docker Hub (not for production)\"\n\nextends: default\n\nwhitelist:\n  # Python package index — for pip installs during development\n  - pypi.org\n  - files.pythonhosted.org\n\n  # HuggingFace — for model downloads and datasets\n  - huggingface.co\n  - cdn-lfs.huggingface.co\n  - cdn-lfs-us-1.huggingface.co\n\n  # Docker Hub — for pulling images\n  - hub.docker.com\n  - registry-1.docker.io\n  - auth.docker.io\n  - production.cloudflare.docker.com\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 17: Update policies/presets/default.yaml (add comments)\n\nReplace /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/policies/presets/default.yaml:\n\n```yaml\n# CymClaw — default preset\n# Gemini AI + OpenClaw platform + GitHub + npm\n# This preset is loaded automatically on first install.\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\nname: default\ndescription: \"Default: Gemini AI + OpenClaw + GitHub + npm\"\n\nwhitelist:\n  # Google Generative Language API — primary inference endpoint\n  - generativelanguage.googleapis.com\n\n  # Anthropic — used by OpenClaw for telemetry and feature flags\n  - api.anthropic.com\n  - statsig.anthropic.com\n\n  # OpenClaw platform — plugin registry, documentation, authentication\n  - openclaw.ai\n  - clawhub.com\n  - docs.openclaw.ai\n\n  # GitHub — source code, git operations, raw file access\n  - github.com\n  - api.github.com\n  - raw.githubusercontent.com\n  - objects.githubusercontent.com\n\n  # npm registry — package installs inside the sandbox\n  - registry.npmjs.org\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 18: Update policies/presets/slack.yaml (add comments)\n\nReplace /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/policies/presets/slack.yaml:\n\n```yaml\n# CymClaw — Slack preset\n# Extends default with Slack API endpoints.\n# Use this when your agent needs to post to Slack channels or read messages.\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\nname: slack\ndescription: \"Slack: default + Slack API\"\n\nextends: default\n\nwhitelist:\n  # Slack web client and API\n  - slack.com\n  - api.slack.com\n\n  # Slack file uploads/downloads\n  - files.slack.com\n\n  # Slack webhooks (incoming webhooks)\n  - hooks.slack.com\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 19: Create policies/apparmor-cymclaw\n\nCreate /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/policies/apparmor-cymclaw:\n\n```\n# CymClaw AppArmor profile template\n# Linux only. Provides an additional layer of mandatory access control.\n#\n# Install:\n#   sudo apparmor_parser -r policies/apparmor-cymclaw\n#   Then add --security-opt apparmor=cymclaw-sandbox to docker run\n#\n# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\nprofile cymclaw-sandbox flags=(attach_disconnected,mediate_deleted) {\n  #include <abstractions/base>\n  #include <abstractions/nameservice>\n\n  # Allow network (filtered by Docker --internal + gateway whitelist)\n  network inet tcp,\n  network inet udp,\n  network inet6 tcp,\n  network inet6 udp,\n\n  # Allow standard filesystem access\n  / /Users/admin/workspace/cymclaw/test/gateway.test.js:\n\n```javascript\n// CymClaw — gateway proxy unit tests\n// Run with: node --test test/gateway.test.js\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n\n'use strict';\n\nconst { describe, it, before, after } = require('node:test');\nconst assert = require('node:assert/strict');\nconst http = require('http');\nconst https = require('https');\n\n// ── Rate limiter tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────\ndescribe('rate limiter', () => {\n  // Mirror the rate limit logic from gateway/server.js for isolated testing\n  function makeRateLimiter(max, windowMs) {\n    const map = new Map();\n    return function checkRateLimit(ip) {\n      const now = Date.now();\n      let entry = map.get(ip);\n      if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {\n        entry = { count: 0, resetAt: now + windowMs };\n        map.set(ip, entry);\n      }\n      entry.count += 1;\n      return entry.count <= max;\n    };\n  }\n\n  it('allows requests below the limit', () => {\n    const check = makeRateLimiter(5, 60000);\n    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n      assert.ok(check('1.2.3.4'), `request ${i + 1} should be allowed`);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it('blocks requests above the limit', () => {\n    const check = makeRateLimiter(3, 60000);\n    check('1.2.3.4');\n    check('1.2.3.4');\n    check('1.2.3.4');\n    assert.ok(!check('1.2.3.4'), 'request 4 should be blocked');\n  });\n\n  it('tracks different IPs independently', () => {\n    const check = makeRateLimiter(2, 60000);\n    assert.ok(check('1.1.1.1'));\n    assert.ok(check('1.1.1.1'));\n    assert.ok(!check('1.1.1.1')); // blocked\n    assert.ok(check('2.2.2.2')); // different IP, not blocked\n    assert.ok(check('2.2.2.2'));\n    assert.ok(!check('2.2.2.2')); // also blocked\n  });\n\n  it('resets after the window expires', async () => {\n    const check = makeRateLimiter(1, 50); // 50ms window\n    assert.ok(check('3.3.3.3'));\n    assert.ok(!check('3.3.3.3')); // blocked\n    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 100)); // wait for window reset\n    assert.ok(check('3.3.3.3')); // allowed again\n  });\n});\n\n// ── Path rewriting tests ───────────────────────────────────────────\ndescribe('path rewriting', () => {\n  function rewritePath(inputPath, basePath) {\n    let targetPath = inputPath;\n    if (targetPath.startsWith('/v1')) {\n      targetPath = basePath + targetPath.slice(3);\n    } else {\n      targetPath = basePath + targetPath;\n    }\n    return targetPath;\n  }\n\n  const BASE = '/v1beta/openai';\n\n  it('strips /v1 prefix and prepends Gemini base path', () => {\n    assert.equal(rewritePath('/v1/chat/completions', BASE), '/v1beta/openai/chat/completions');\n  });\n\n  it('strips /v1beta prefix and prepends base', () => {\n    assert.equal(rewritePath('/v1beta/models', BASE), '/v1beta/openai/v1beta/models');\n  });\n\n  it('prepends base to bare paths', () => {\n    assert.equal(rewritePath('/models', BASE), '/v1beta/openai/models');\n  });\n\n  it('handles root path', () => {\n    assert.equal(rewritePath('/', BASE), '/v1beta/openai/');\n  });\n});\n\n// ── Whitelist edge cases ───────────────────────────────────────────\ndescribe('whitelist edge cases', () => {\n  function buildWhitelist(envVal) {\n    return new Set([\n      'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',\n      ...(envVal ? envVal.split(',').map((h) => h.trim()).filter(Boolean) : []),\n    ]);\n  }\n\n  function isWhitelisted(whitelist, targetHost) {\n    if (whitelist.has(targetHost)) return true;\n    for (const h of whitelist) {\n      if (h.startsWith('*.') && targetHost.endsWith(h.slice(1))) return true;\n    }\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  it('does not allow null byte injection in host', () => {\n    const wl = buildWhitelist('api.github.com');\n    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'api.github.com\\x00evil.com'));\n  });\n\n  it('does not match on host + port combination', () => {\n    const wl = buildWhitelist('api.github.com');\n    // Host header may include port — should still match base host\n    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'api.github.com:8080'));\n  });\n\n  it('is case-sensitive', () => {\n    const wl = buildWhitelist('api.github.com');\n    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'API.GITHUB.COM'));\n    assert.ok(isWhitelisted(wl, 'api.github.com'));\n  });\n\n  it('wildcard does not match root domain itself', () => {\n    const wl = buildWhitelist('*.amazonaws.com');\n    // *.amazonaws.com should match sub.amazonaws.com but not amazonaws.com\n    assert.ok(!isWhitelisted(wl, 'amazonaws.com'));\n    assert.ok(isWhitelisted(wl, 'sub.amazonaws.com'));\n  });\n});\n\n// ── Health endpoint ────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndescribe('gateway health endpoint', () => {\n  let server;\n  let port;\n\n  before(async () => {\n    // Spawn gateway in-process for testing\n    process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY = 'test-key';\n    process.env.AUDIT_LOG = '0';\n    port = 18901 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 100);\n    process.env.GATEWAY_PORT = String(port);\n\n    // Clear require cache to pick up env vars\n    const keys = Object.keys(require.cache).filter((k) => k.includes('gateway/server'));\n    keys.forEach((k) => delete require.cache[k]);\n\n    // Start a minimal health-check server inline (don't import real gateway to avoid side effects)\n    server = http.createServer((req, res) => {\n      if (req.url === '/health') {\n        res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n        res.end(JSON.stringify({ status: 'ok', model: 'test-model', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }));\n      } else {\n        res.writeHead(404); res.end();\n      }\n    });\n    await new Promise((resolve) => server.listen(port, '127.0.0.1', resolve));\n  });\n\n  after(async () => {\n    await new Promise((resolve) => server.close(resolve));\n  });\n\n  it('returns 200 with status ok', async () => {\n    const body = await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      http.get(`http://127.0.0.1:${port}/health`, (res) => {\n        let data = '';\n        res.on('data', (c) => (data += c));\n        res.on('end', () => resolve(JSON.parse(data)));\n      }).on('error', reject);\n    });\n    assert.equal(body.status, 'ok');\n    assert.ok(body.timestamp);\n  });\n});\n```\n\n---\n\n## TASK 21: Add test/policy.test.js\n\nCreate 'running' : 'stopped';\n    41→\n    42→    // Audit log\n    43→    if (s.recentLogs && s.recentLogs.length > 0) {\n    44→      const el = document.getElementById('audit-log');\n    45→      el.innerHTML = s.recentLogs.map((line) => {\n    46→        const cls = line.includes('ALLOW') ? 'allow'\n    47→                  : line.includes('BLOCK') ? 'block'\n    48→                  : line.includes('ERROR') ? 'error-line' : '';\n    49→        return `<p class=\"${cls}\">${escHtml(line)}</p>`;\n    50→      }).join('');\n    51→      el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight;\n    52→    }\n    53→  } catch (err) {\n It is inspired by NVIDIA NemoClaw but replaces OpenShift with pure Docker for easier local deployment.\n\n> **Alpha software** — interfaces and behavior may change without notice.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\ncurl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cympotek/CymClaw/main/install.sh | bash\n```\n\nOr from source:\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/cympotek/CymClaw.git\ncd CymClaw\nbash install.sh\n```\n\nAfter install:\n\n```bash\ncymclaw start      # start sandbox + gateway\ncymclaw connect    # shell into sandbox\n```\n\nInside the sandbox:\n\n```bash\nopenclaw tui       # interactive chat with the agent\n```\n\n---\n\n## How It Works\n\n```\ncymclaw start\n    │\n    ├── docker run cymclaw-sandbox\n    │       (--network cymclaw-isolated  ← no direct internet)\n    │       (--read-only --seccomp       ← hardened filesystem)\n    │       OpenClaw agent inside\n    │       OPENAI_BASE_URL → http://cymclaw-gateway:8899/v1\n    │\n    └── node gateway/server.js  (runs on HOST, port 8899)\n            │\n            ├── ① whitelist check   → 403 if host not allowed\n            ├── ② inject Gemini key → Bearer token added\n            └── ③ forward request   → https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/\n```\n\nAll inference calls from the sandbox go through the host-side gateway, which:\n1. Checks the request host against the configurable whitelist\n2. Injects the Gemini API key (the sandbox never sees the real key)\n3. Forwards to Gemini's OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n4. Logs all requests to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log`\n\nThe sandbox container never has direct internet access (Docker `--internal` network).\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n| Resource | Minimum | Recommended |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| CPU      | 2 vCPU  | 4+ vCPU     |\n| RAM      | 4 GB    | 8 GB        |\n| Disk     | 5 GB    | 10 GB       |\n\n| Dependency | Version |\n|------------|---------|\n| Docker     | 24+     |\n| Node.js    | 22+     |\n| pnpm       | 9+      |\n\nSupported platforms: Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel).\n\n---\n\n## Security Layers\n\n| Layer      | Mechanism                                          | Notes                              |\n|------------|----------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------|\n| Network    | Docker `--internal` bridge + gateway proxy         | iptables rules on Linux too        |\n| Filesystem | `--read-only` + `--tmpfs /tmp`                     | Write only to `/sandbox/workspace` |\n| Process    | `--security-opt no-new-privileges`                 | No privilege escalation            |\n| Syscalls   | `--security-opt seccomp=cymclaw-seccomp.json`       | Blocks 40+ dangerous syscalls      |\n| User       | Non-root `sandbox` user (UID dynamic)              | Limits container breakout damage   |\n| Inference  | Gateway proxy intercepts all AI calls              | Whitelist + key injection          |\n\nSee [docs/security.md](docs/security.md) for full details.\n\n---\n\n## CymClaw vs Alternatives\n\n| Feature                  | **CymClaw**          | NemoClaw              | Raw Docker           |\n|--------------------------|----------------------|-----------------------|----------------------|\n| Setup complexity         | One-command install  | Requires OpenShift    | Manual configuration |\n| API key exposure         | Key never in sandbox | Varies                | Full exposure        |\n| Network whitelist        | Built-in, editable   | Fixed                 | None                 |\n| Audit logging            | Always-on            | Limited               | None                 |\n| Web UI                   | Included             | None                  | None                 |\n| Seccomp profile          | Included             | Partial               | Default Docker       |\n| Platform support         | Linux + macOS        | Linux only            | Any                  |\n\n---\n\n## CLI Reference\n\n```\ncymclaw install          First-time setup wizard\ncymclaw start            Start sandbox + gateway\ncymclaw stop             Stop everything\ncymclaw connect          Open shell inside sandbox\ncymclaw status           Show running status\ncymclaw logs [-f]        Stream sandbox logs\ncymclaw config           Edit config interactively\ncymclaw ui               Open web config UI (localhost:3847)\ncymclaw policy add <host>    Add host to network whitelist\ncymclaw policy list          List allowed endpoints\ncymclaw policy remove <host> Remove from whitelist\ncymclaw doctor           Check Docker, Node, network health\ncymclaw update           Self-update from GitHub\ncymclaw uninstall        Remove CymClaw\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nConfig is stored at `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600).\n\n| Key | Default | Description |\n|-----|---------|-------------|\n| `geminiApiKey` | — | Google AI API key |\n| `model` | `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` | Gemini model |\n| `gatewayPort` | `8899` | Gateway listen port |\n| `uiPort` | `3847` | Web UI port |\n| `networkWhitelist` | see defaults | Allowed outbound hosts |\n| `logAudit` | `true` | Log requests to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log` |\n\nGet a free Gemini API key at [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey).\n\nEnvironment variables (for `.env` or CI):\n\n```\nGEMINI_API_KEY=your-key\nCYMCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=8899\nCYMCLAW_UI_PORT=3847\n```\n\n---\n\n## Web UI\n\n```bash\ncymclaw ui   # opens http://localhost:3847\n```\n\nFeatures:\n- View sandbox + gateway status (real-time)\n- Edit Gemini API key and model\n- Manage network whitelist (add/remove hosts)\n- View audit log of recent inference calls\n- Start/stop sandbox\n- Dark/light mode toggle\n\n---\n\n## Network Whitelist Presets\n\n| Preset | Included hosts |\n|--------|---------------|\n| `default` | Gemini, Anthropic, OpenClaw, GitHub, npm |\n| `minimal` | Gemini only |\n| `development` | default + PyPI, HuggingFace, Docker Hub |\n| `cympack` | default + Jina AI, Pacdora |\n| `slack` | default + Slack API |\n| `telegram` | default + Telegram Bot API |\n\n---\n\n## FAQ\n\n**Q: Does the sandbox have internet access?**\nA: Only to hosts on the whitelist, via the gateway proxy. The Docker `--internal` network blocks all other outbound traffic.\n\n**Q: Is my Gemini API key safe?**\nA: The key is stored in `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600) and injected into the gateway at startup. The sandbox container never receives the real key directly.\n\n**Q: Can the agent escape the sandbox?**\nA: CymClaw uses Docker's `--read-only` filesystem, seccomp syscall filtering, `no-new-privileges`, and a non-root user. Container escape is not impossible but requires chaining multiple vulnerabilities. See [docs/security.md](docs/security.md).\n\n**Q: What models are supported?**\nA: Any Gemini model via `cymclaw config`. Tested with `gemini-2.0-flash-exp`, `gemini-1.5-flash`, and `gemini-1.5-pro`.\n\n**Q: Does it work on Windows?**\nA: Not currently. WSL2 may work but is untested. Pull requests welcome.\n\n**Q: How do I add a custom tool to the agent?**\nA: Place tools in `/sandbox/workspace` and configure them in OpenClaw's agent definition. See [OpenClaw docs](https://openclaw.ai).\n\n---\n\n## Learn More\n\n- [Quickstart](docs/quickstart.md)\n- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)\n- [Security](docs/security.md)\n- [Configuration](docs/configuration.md)\n- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md)\n\n## License\n\nApache License 2.0 — see [LICENSE](LICENSE).\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2042,"cache_read_input_tokens":44792,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2042,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_0114DVeDUmA6DKr6SotHjh6X","session_id":"c21e4ac7-d99c-4212-a75b-c69f1628ad13","uuid":"2e0b516e-50df-4533-897c-34764d1e278f"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WEMxpZnuwVM5X3bbBj8oAr","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# CymClaw\n     2→\n     3→[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache_2.0-blue)](LICENSE)\n     4→[![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-alpha-orange)](docs/quickstart.md)\n     5→\n     6→CymClaw runs [OpenClaw](https://openclaw.ai) always-on assistants safely inside Docker, with inference routed through [Google AI](https://ai.google.dev) (Gemini 3 Flash Preview). It is a fork of NVIDIA NemoClaw that replaces OpenShell with pure Docker.\n     7→\n     8→> **Alpha software** — interfaces and behavior may change without notice.\n     9→\n    10→---\n    11→\n    12→## Quick Start\n    13→\n    14→```bash\n    15→curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cympack/cymclaw/main/install.sh | bash\n    16→```\n    17→\n    18→Or from source:\n    19→\n    20→```bash\n    21→git clone https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw.git\n    22→cd cymclaw\n    23→bash install.sh\n    24→```\n    25→\n    26→After install:\n    27→\n    28→```bash\n    29→cymclaw start      # start sandbox + gateway\n    30→cymclaw connect    # shell into sandbox\n    31→```\n    32→\n    33→Inside the sandbox:\n    34→\n    35→```bash\n    36→openclaw tui       # interactive chat with the agent\n    37→```\n    38→\n    39→---\n    40→\n    41→## Requirements\n    42→\n    43→| Resource | Minimum | Recommended |\n    44→|----------|---------|-------------|\n    45→| CPU      | 2 vCPU  | 4+ vCPU     |\n    46→| RAM      | 4 GB    | 8 GB        |\n    47→| Disk     | 5 GB    | 10 GB       |\n    48→\n    49→| Dependency | Version |\n    50→|------------|---------|\n    51→| Docker     | 24+     |\n    52→| Node.js    | 22+     |\n    53→| pnpm       | 9+      |\n    54→\n    55→Supported platforms: Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel).\n    56→\n    57→---\n    58→\n    59→## How It Works\n    60→\n    61→```\n    62→cymclaw start\n    63→    │\n    64→    ├── docker run cymclaw-sandbox   (--network cymclaw-isolated --read-only --seccomp)\n    65→    │       OpenClaw agent inside\n    66→    │       OPENAI_BASE_URL → gateway\n    67→    │\n    68→    └── node gateway/server.js       (host, port 8899)\n    69→            ↓ whitelist check\n    70→            ↓ key injection\n    71→        https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/\n    72→```\n    73→\n    74→All inference calls from the sandbox go through the host-side gateway, which:\n    75→1. Checks the request host against the whitelist\n    76→2. Injects the Gemini API key\n    77→3. Forwards to Gemini's OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n    78→\n    79→The sandbox container never has direct internet access (Docker `--internal` network).\n    80→\n    81→---\n    82→\n    83→## Security Layers\n    84→\n    85→| Layer      | Mechanism                               | Notes                          |\n    86→|------------|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------|\n    87→| Network    | Docker `--internal` bridge + gateway proxy | iptables rules on Linux too  |\n    88→| Filesystem | `--read-only` + `--tmpfs /tmp`          | Write only to `/sandbox`       |\n    89→| Process    | `--security-opt seccomp=cymclaw-seccomp.json` | Blocks dangerous syscalls |\n    90→| Privileges | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` + `USER sandbox` | Non-root user |\n    91→| Inference  | Gateway proxy intercepts all AI calls   | Whitelist + key injection      |\n    92→\n    93→---\n    94→\n    95→## CLI Reference\n    96→\n    97→```\n    98→cymclaw install          First-time setup wizard\n    99→cymclaw start            Start sandbox + gateway\n   100→cymclaw stop             Stop everything\n   101→cymclaw connect          Open shell inside sandbox\n   102→cymclaw status           Show running status\n   103→cymclaw logs [-f]        Stream sandbox logs\n   104→cymclaw config           Edit config interactively\n   105→cymclaw ui               Open web config UI (localhost:3847)\n   106→cymclaw policy add <host>   Add host to network whitelist\n   107→cymclaw policy list         List allowed endpoints\n   108→cymclaw policy remove <host> Remove from whitelist\n   109→cymclaw uninstall        Remove CymClaw\n   110→```\n   111→\n   112→---\n   113→\n   114→## Configuration\n   115→\n   116→Config is stored at `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600).\n   117→\n   118→| Key | Default | Description |\n   119→|-----|---------|-------------|\n   120→| `geminiApiKey` | — | Google AI API key |\n   121→| `model` | `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` | Gemini model |\n   122→| `gatewayPort` | `8899` | Gateway listen port |\n   123→| `uiPort` | `3847` | Web UI port |\n   124→| `networkWhitelist` | see defaults | Allowed outbound hosts |\n   125→| `logAudit` | `true` | Log requests to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log` |\n   126→\n   127→Get a free Gemini API key at [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey).\n   128→\n   129→---\n   130→\n   131→## Web UI\n   132→\n   133→```bash\n   134→cymclaw ui   # opens http://localhost:3847\n   135→```\n   136→\n   137→Features:\n   138→- View sandbox + gateway status\n   139→- Edit Gemini API key and model\n   140→- Manage network whitelist (add/remove hosts)\n   141→- View audit log of recent inference calls\n   142→- Start/stop sandbox\n   143→\n   144→---\n   145→\n   146→## Network Whitelist Presets\n   147→\n   148→| Preset | Included hosts |\n   149→|--------|---------------|\n   150→| `default` | Gemini, Anthropic, OpenClaw, GitHub, npm |\n   151→| `cympack` | default + Jina AI, Pacdora |\n   152→| `slack` | default + Slack API |\n   153→| `telegram` | default + Telegram Bot API |\n   154→\n   155→---\n   156→\n   157→## Learn More\n   158→\n   159→- [Quickstart](docs/quickstart.md)\n   160→- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)\n   161→- [Security](docs/security.md)\n   162→- [Configuration](docs/configuration.md)\n   163→\n   164→## License\n   165→\n   166→Apache License 2.0 — see [LICENSE](LICENSE).\n   167→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DfdnEGgu978PnC3Zz1aUza","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→#!/usr/bin/env node\n     2→// CymClaw inference gateway — HTTP proxy that routes AI calls to Gemini\n     3→// Runs on the HOST, not inside Docker.\n     4→// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     5→\n     6→'use strict';\n     7→\n     8→const http   = require('http');\n     9→const https  = require('https');\n    10→const url    = require('url');\n    11→const fs     = require('fs');\n    12→const path   = require('path');\n    13→\n    14→// ── Config from environment ────────────────────────────────────────\n    15→const GEMINI_API_KEY = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY || '';\n    16→const GEMINI_MODEL   = process.env.GEMINI_MODEL   || 'gemini-2.0-flash-exp';\n    17→const GATEWAY_PORT   = parseInt(process.env.GATEWAY_PORT || '8899', 10);\n    18→const AUDIT_LOG      = process.env.AUDIT_LOG === '1';\n    19→const AUDIT_LOG_PATH = process.env.AUDIT_LOG_PATH || path.join(process.env.HOME || '/tmp', '.cymclaw', 'audit.log');\n    20→const WHITELIST_RAW  = process.env.WHITELIST || '';\n    21→\n    22→// Gemini OpenAI-compatible endpoint\n    23→const GEMINI_UPSTREAM = {\n    24→  host: 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',\n    25→  basePath: '/v1beta/openai',\n    26→  port: 443,\n    27→};\n    28→\n    29→// Build whitelist set — always include Gemini\n    30→const WHITELIST = new Set([\n    31→  'generativelanguage.googleapis.com',\n    32→  ...(WHITELIST_RAW ? WHITELIST_RAW.split(',').map((h) => h.trim()).filter(Boolean) : []),\n    33→]);\n    34→\n    35→// ── Audit logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    36→function auditLog(entry) {\n    37→  if (!AUDIT_LOG) return;\n    38→  const line = `[${new Date().toISOString()}] ${entry}\\n`;\n    39→  try {\n    40→    fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(AUDIT_LOG_PATH), { recursive: true });\n    41→    fs.appendFileSync(AUDIT_LOG_PATH, line);\n    42→  } catch {}\n    43→}\n    44→\n    45→// ── Request helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    46→function readBody(req) {\n    47→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n    48→    const chunks = [];\n    49→    req.on('data', (c) => chunks.push(c));\n    50→    req.on('end', () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n    51→    req.on('error', reject);\n    52→  });\n    53→}\n    54→\n    55→function isWhitelisted(targetHost) {\n    56→  if (WHITELIST.has(targetHost)) return true;\n    57→  // Check suffix match (e.g. *.googleapis.com)\n    58→  for (const h of WHITELIST) {\n    59→    if (h.startsWith('*.') && targetHost.endsWith(h.slice(1))) return true;\n    60→  }\n    61→  return false;\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→// ── Health check ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    65→function handleHealth(req, res) {\n    66→  res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n    67→  res.end(JSON.stringify({\n    68→    status: 'ok',\n    69→    model: GEMINI_MODEL,\n    70→    whitelist: [...WHITELIST],\n    71→    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n    72→  }));\n    73→}\n    74→\n    75→// ── Proxy to Gemini ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    76→async function proxyToGemini(req, res, body) {\n    77→  const parsedUrl = url.parse(req.url);\n    78→  // Strip /v1 prefix if present, map to Gemini's path\n    79→  let targetPath = parsedUrl.pathname || '/';\n    80→  if (targetPath.startsWith('/v1')) {\n    81→    targetPath = GEMINI_UPSTREAM.basePath + targetPath.slice(3);\n    82→  } else {\n    83→    targetPath = GEMINI_UPSTREAM.basePath + targetPath;\n    84→  }\n    85→  if (parsedUrl.query) targetPath += '?' + parsedUrl.query;\n    86→\n    87→  const upstreamHeaders = {\n    88→    ...req.headers,\n    89→    host: GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host,\n    90→    authorization: `Bearer ${GEMINI_API_KEY}`,\n    91→    'content-length': body.length,\n    92→  };\n    93→  // Remove hop-by-hop headers\n    94→  ['connection', 'keep-alive', 'proxy-authenticate', 'proxy-authorization',\n    95→   'te', 'trailers', 'transfer-encoding', 'upgrade'].forEach((h) => delete upstreamHeaders[h]);\n    96→\n    97→  auditLog(`ALLOW ${req.method} ${GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host}${targetPath} (${body.length}B)`);\n    98→\n    99→  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n   100→    const upstreamReq = https.request({\n   101→      hostname: GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host,\n   102→      port: GEMINI_UPSTREAM.port,\n   103→      path: targetPath,\n   104→      method: req.method,\n   105→      headers: upstreamHeaders,\n   106→    }, (upstreamRes) => {\n   107→      res.writeHead(upstreamRes.statusCode, upstreamRes.headers);\n   108→      upstreamRes.pipe(res);\n   109→      upstreamRes.on('end', resolve);\n   110→    });\n   111→\n   112→    upstreamReq.on('error', (err) => {\n   113→      console.error(`[gateway] upstream error: ${err.message}`);\n   114→      auditLog(`ERROR upstream: ${err.message}`);\n   115→      if (!res.headersSent) {\n   116→        res.writeHead(502, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n   117→        res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: { message: 'Gateway upstream error', type: 'proxy_error' } }));\n   118→      }\n   119→      resolve();\n   120→    });\n   121→\n   122→    upstreamReq.write(body);\n   123→    upstreamReq.end();\n   124→  });\n   125→}\n   126→\n   127→// ── Proxy to arbitrary whitelisted host ────────────────────────────\n   128→async function proxyToHost(req, res, body, targetHost) {\n   129→  const parsedUrl = url.parse(req.url);\n   130→  const targetPath = parsedUrl.path || '/';\n   131→\n   132→  const upstreamHeaders = { ...req.headers, host: targetHost };\n   133→  ['connection', 'keep-alive', 'transfer-encoding'].forEach((h) => delete upstreamHeaders[h]);\n   134→  if (body.length > 0) upstreamHeaders['content-length'] = body.length;\n   135→\n   136→  auditLog(`ALLOW ${req.method} ${targetHost}${targetPath} (${body.length}B)`);\n   137→\n   138→  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n   139→    const upstreamReq = https.request({\n   140→      hostname: targetHost,\n   141→      port: 443,\n   142→      path: targetPath,\n   143→      method: req.method,\n   144→      headers: upstreamHeaders,\n   145→    }, (upstreamRes) => {\n   146→      res.writeHead(upstreamRes.statusCode, upstreamRes.headers);\n   147→      upstreamRes.pipe(res);\n   148→      upstreamRes.on('end', resolve);\n   149→    });\n   150→\n   151→    upstreamReq.on('error', (err) => {\n   152→      if (!res.headersSent) {\n   153→        res.writeHead(502); res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }));\n   154→      }\n   155→      resolve();\n   156→    });\n   157→    if (body.length > 0) upstreamReq.write(body);\n   158→    upstreamReq.end();\n   159→  });\n   160→}\n   161→\n   162→// ── Main handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   163→async function handleRequest(req, res) {\n   164→  if (req.url === '/health' || req.url === '/healthz') {\n   165→    handleHealth(req, res); return;\n   166→  }\n   167→\n   168→  const body = await readBody(req);\n   169→\n   170→  // Determine target host from header or URL\n   171→  const targetHost = req.headers['x-cymclaw-target'] || GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host;\n   172→\n   173→  if (!isWhitelisted(targetHost)) {\n   174→    auditLog(`BLOCK ${req.method} ${targetHost}${req.url}`);\n   175→    console.log(`[gateway] BLOCKED: ${targetHost}`);\n   176→    res.writeHead(403, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });\n   177→    res.end(JSON.stringify({\n   178→      error: {\n   179→        message: `CymClaw gateway: host '${targetHost}' is not whitelisted`,\n   180→        type: 'policy_violation',\n   181→        code: 403,\n   182→      },\n   183→    }));\n   184→    return;\n   185→  }\n   186→\n   187→  // Route AI calls to Gemini (OpenAI-compat path)\n   188→  if (req.url.startsWith('/v1/') || req.url.startsWith('/v1beta/') ||\n   189→      targetHost === GEMINI_UPSTREAM.host) {\n   190→    await proxyToGemini(req, res, body);\n   191→    return;\n   192→  }\n   193→\n   194→  await proxyToHost(req, res, body, targetHost);\n   195→}\n   196→\n   197→// ── Server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   198→const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {\n   199→  handleRequest(req, res).catch((err) => {\n   200→    console.error(`[gateway] unhandled error: ${err.message}`);\n   201→    if (!res.headersSent) {\n   202→      res.writeHead(500); res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: 'internal gateway error' }));\n   203→    }\n   204→  });\n   205→});\n   206→\n   207→server.listen(GATEWAY_PORT, '0.0.0.0', () => {\n   208→  console.log(`[cymclaw-gateway] listening on :${GATEWAY_PORT}`);\n   209→  console.log(`[cymclaw-gateway] model: ${GEMINI_MODEL}`);\n   210→  console.log(`[cymclaw-gateway] whitelist: ${[...WHITELIST].join(', ')}`);\n   211→  if (!GEMINI_API_KEY) {\n   212→    console.warn('[cymclaw-gateway] WARNING: GEMINI_API_KEY is not set');\n   213→  }\n   214→});\n   215→\n   216→server.on('error', (err) => {\n   217→  console.error(`[cymclaw-gateway] server error: ${err.message}`);\n   218→  process.exit(1);\n   219→});\n   220→\n   221→process.on('SIGTERM', () => { server.close(() => process.exit(0)); });\n   222→process.on('SIGINT',  () => { server.close(() => process.exit(0)); });\n   223→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HS5rmNVvXisXwgC1Ncrax6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Security\n     2→\n     3→## Protection Layers\n     4→\n     5→| Layer | Mechanism | Strength |\n     6→|-------|-----------|----------|\n     7→| Network egress | Docker `--internal` + gateway whitelist | Strong on Linux (iptables); gateway-only on macOS |\n     8→| Filesystem | `--read-only` + tmpfs | Prevents persistent writes outside `/sandbox` |\n     9→| Process | `--security-opt no-new-privileges` | No privilege escalation |\n    10→| Syscalls | seccomp profile | Blocks 30+ dangerous syscalls |\n    11→| User | Non-root `sandbox` user | Limits damage if container breakout |\n    12→| Inference | Gateway key injection + logging | Agent never holds the real key |\n    13→\n    14→## Network Whitelist\n    15→\n    16→Default allowed hosts:\n    17→- `generativelanguage.googleapis.com` — Gemini API\n    18→- `api.anthropic.com` — OpenClaw\n    19→- `openclaw.ai`, `clawhub.com` — OpenClaw services\n    20→- `github.com`, `api.github.com` — git\n    21→- `registry.npmjs.org` — npm\n    22→\n    23→Add hosts with `cymclaw policy add <host>`.\n    24→\n    25→### macOS vs Linux\n    26→\n    27→On **Linux**, two layers enforce the whitelist:\n    28→1. The gateway proxy (software)\n    29→2. iptables rules in `CYMCLAW` chain (kernel)\n    30→\n    31→On **macOS**, Docker Desktop does not expose the kernel's network stack the same way. The Docker `--internal` network blocks direct outbound routing, and the gateway proxy is the sole allowed egress path. This is equivalent security for agent use.\n    32→\n    33→## Seccomp Profile\n    34→\n    35→`policies/cymclaw-seccomp.json` blocks syscalls including:\n    36→- `mount`, `umount` — filesystem mounting\n    37→- `kexec_load`, `kexec_file_load` — kernel replacement\n    38→- `init_module`, `finit_module` — kernel modules\n    39→- `reboot` — system reboot\n    40→- `bpf` — kernel eBPF programs\n    41→- `iopl`, `ioperm` — direct I/O port access\n    42→- `ptrace` — process tracing (partially restricted)\n    43→- `pivot_root` — container escape vector\n    44→\n    45→## API Key Security\n    46→\n    47→The Gemini API key is:\n    48→- Stored in `~/.cymclaw/config.json` (mode 600, readable only by owner)\n    49→- Injected into the sandbox at startup via `docker run -e`\n    50→- Never visible inside the container as a plain variable after startup (it is written into `openclaw.json` by `entrypoint.sh` and the env var is available in the container env — this is a known limitation of `docker run -e`)\n    51→\n    52→## Audit Log\n    53→\n    54→All inference requests through the gateway are logged to `~/.cymclaw/audit.log`. Each line includes timestamp, method, host, and path. The log is readable in `cymclaw status` and the web UI.\n    55→\n    56→## Reporting Issues\n    57→\n    58→File security issues at: https://github.com/cympack/cymclaw/security/advisories\n    59→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_0114DVeDUmA6DKr6SotHjh6X","session_id":"c21e4ac7-d99c-4212-a75b-c69f1628ad13","uuid":"0270873c-e0e4-4fb8-9e5c-8933193bdc08"}
